Psalms8:1 For the choir director; on the gittith; a psalm by David. O Lord, our Lord, how majestic is your name throughout the earth! Your glory is sung above the heavens." 
Psalms8:2 From the mouths of little children and infants, you have built a fortress against your opponents to silence the enemy and the avenger." 
Psalms8:3 When I look at your heavens, the creation of your fingers, the moon and the stars that you have set in place—" 
Psalms8:4 what is a mortal that you remember him or the Son of Man that you take care of him? 
Psalms8:5 You have made him a little lower than yourself. You have crowned him with glory and honor. 
Psalms8:6 You have made him rule what your hands created. You have put everything under his control: 
Psalms8:7 all the sheep and cattle, the wild animals," 
Psalms8:8 the birds, the fish, whatever swims in the currents of the seas." 
Psalms8:9 O Lord, our Lord, how majestic is your name throughout the earth!" 
Psalms9:1 For the choir director; according to muth labben; a psalm by David. I will give {you} thanks, O Lord, with all my heart. I will tell about all the miracles you have done." 
Psalms9:2 I will find joy and be glad about you. I will make music to praise your name, O Most High." 
Psalms9:3 When my enemies retreat, they will stumble and die in your presence." 
Psalms9:4 You have defended my just cause: You sat down on your throne as a fair judge. 
Psalms9:5 You condemned nations. You destroyed wicked people. You wiped out their names forever and ever. 
Psalms9:6 The enemy is finished—in ruins forever. You have uprooted their cities. Even the memory of them has faded. 
Psalms9:7 Yet, the Lord is enthroned forever. He has set up his throne for judgment." 
Psalms9:8 He alone judges the world with righteousness. He judges {its} people fairly. 
Psalms9:9 The Lord is a stronghold for the oppressed, a stronghold in times of trouble." 
Psalms9:10 Those who know your name trust you, O Lord, because you have never deserted those who seek your help." 
Psalms9:11 Make music to praise the Lord, who is enthroned in Zion. Announce to the nations what he has done." 
Psalms9:12 The one who avenges murder has remembered oppressed people. He has never forgotten their cries. 
Psalms9:13 Have pity on me, O Lord. Look at what I suffer because of those who hate me. You take me away from the gates of death" 
Psalms9:14 so that I may recite your praises one by one in the gates of Zion and find joy in your salvation. 
Psalms9:15 The nations have sunk into the pit they have made. Their feet are caught in the net they have hidden {to trap others}. 
Psalms9:16 The Lord is known by the judgment he has carried out. The wicked person is trapped by the work of his own hands. Higgaion Selah 
Psalms9:17 Wicked people, all the nations who forget God, will return to the grave." 
Psalms9:18 Needy people will not always be forgotten. Nor will the hope of oppressed people be lost forever. 
Psalms9:19 Arise, O Lord. Do not let mortals gain any power. Let the nations be judged in your presence." 
Psalms9:20 Strike them with terror, O Lord. Let the nations know that they are {only} mortal. Selah" 
Psalms10:1 Why are you so distant, Lord? Why do you hide yourself in times of trouble?" 
Psalms10:2 The wicked person arrogantly pursues oppressed people. He will be caught in the schemes that he planned. 
Psalms10:3 The wicked person boasts about his selfish desires. He blesses robbers, but he curses the Lord." 
Psalms10:4 He turns up his nose {and says}, ""God doesn't care."" His every thought {concludes}, ""There is no God.""" 
Psalms10:5 He always seems to succeed. Your judgments are beyond his understanding. He spits at all his opponents. 
Psalms10:6 He says to himself, ""Nothing can shake me. I'll never face any trouble.""" 
Psalms10:7 His mouth is full of cursing, deception, and oppression. Trouble and wrongdoing are on the tip of his tongue." 
Psalms10:8 He waits in ambush in the villages. From his hiding places he kills innocent people. His eyes are on the lookout for victims. 
Psalms10:9 He lies in his hiding place like a lion in his den. He hides there to catch oppressed people. He catches oppressed people when he draws them into his net. 
Psalms10:10 {His} victims are crushed. They collapse, and they fall under {the weight of} his power." 
Psalms10:11 He says to himself, ""God has forgotten. He has hidden his face. He will never see it!""" 
Psalms10:12 Arise, O Lord! Lift your hand, O God. Do not forget oppressed people!" 
Psalms10:13 Why does the wicked person despise God? Why does he say to himself, ""God doesn't care""?" 
Psalms10:14 You have seen {it}; yes, you have taken note of trouble and grief and placed them under your control. The victim entrusts himself to you. You alone have been the helper of orphans." 
Psalms10:15 Break the arm of the wicked and evil person. Punish his wickedness until you find no more. 
Psalms10:16 The Lord is king forever and ever. The nations have vanished from his land. 
Psalms10:17 You have heard the desire of oppressed people, O Lord. You encourage them. You pay close attention to them" 
Psalms10:18 in order to provide justice for orphans and oppressed people so that no mere mortal will terrify them again. 
Psalms11:1 For the choir director; by David. I have taken refuge in the Lord. How can you say to me: Flee to your mountain like a bird? 
Psalms11:2 Wicked people bend their bows. They set their arrows against the strings to shoot in the dark at people whose motives are decent. 
Psalms11:3 When the foundations {of life} are undermined, what can a righteous person do?""" 
Psalms11:4 The Lord is in his holy temple. The Lord's throne is in heaven. His eyes see. They examine Adam's descendants. 
Psalms11:5 The Lord tests righteous people, but he hates wicked people and the ones who love violence." 
Psalms11:6 He rains down fire and burning sulfur upon wicked people. He makes them drink from a cup filled with scorching wind. 
Psalms11:7 The Lord is righteous. He loves a righteous way of life. Decent people will see his face. 
Psalms12:1 For the choir director; on the sheminith; a psalm by David. Help, O Lord. No godly person is left. Faithful people have vanished from among Adam's descendants!" 
Psalms12:2 All people speak foolishly. They speak with flattering lips. They say one thing but mean another. 
Psalms12:3 May the Lord cut off every flattering lip and every bragging tongue 
Psalms12:4 that has said, ""We will overcome with our tongues. With lips such as ours, who can be our master?""" 
Psalms12:5 Because oppressed people are robbed and needy people groan, I will now arise,"" says the Lord. ""I will provide safety for those who long for it.""" 
Psalms12:6 The promises of the Lord are pure, like silver refined in a furnace and purified seven times." 
Psalms12:7 O Lord, you will protect them. You will keep each one safe from those people forever." 
Psalms12:8 Wicked people parade around when immorality increases among Adam's descendants. 
Psalms13:1 For the choir director; a psalm by David. How long, O Lord? Will you forget me forever? How long will you hide your face from me?" 
Psalms13:2 How long must I make decisions alone with sorrow in my heart day after day? How long will my enemy triumph over me? 
Psalms13:3 Look at me! Answer me, O Lord my God! Light up my eyes, or else I will die" 
Psalms13:4 and my enemy will say, ""I have overpowered him."" My opponents will rejoice because I have been shaken." 
Psalms13:5 But I trust your mercy. My heart finds joy in your salvation. 
Psalms13:6 I will sing to the Lord because he has been good to me. 
Psalms14:1 For the choir director; by David. Godless fools say in their hearts, ""There is no God."" They are corrupt. They do disgusting things. There is no one who does good things." 
Psalms14:2 The Lord looks down from heaven on Adam's descendants to see if there is anyone who acts wisely, if there is anyone who seeks help from God." 
Psalms14:3 Everyone has turned away. Together they have become rotten to the core. No one, not even one person, does good things." 
Psalms14:4 Are all those troublemakers, those who devour my people as if they were devouring food, so ignorant that they do not call on the Lord?" 
Psalms14:5 There they are—panic-stricken because God is with the person who is righteous. 
Psalms14:6 They put the advice of oppressed people to shame because the Lord is their refuge. 
Psalms14:7 If only salvation for Israel would come from Zion! When the Lord restores the fortunes of his people, Jacob will rejoice. Israel will be glad." 
Psalms15:1 A psalm by David. O Lord, who may stay in your tent? Who may live on your holy mountain?" 
Psalms15:2 The one who walks with integrity, does what is righteous, and speaks the truth within his heart." 
Psalms15:3 The one who does not slander with his tongue, do evil to a friend, or bring disgrace on his neighbor." 
Psalms15:4 The one who despises those rejected by God but honors those who fear the Lord. The one who makes a promise and does not break it, even though he is hurt by it." 
Psalms15:5 The one who does not collect interest on a loan or take a bribe against an innocent person. Whoever does these things will never be shaken. 
Psalms16:1 A miktam by David. Protect me, O God, because I take refuge in you." 
Psalms16:2 I said to the Lord, ""You are my Lord. Without you, I have nothing good.""" 
Psalms16:3 Those who lead holy lives on earth are the noble ones who fill me with joy. 
Psalms16:4 Those who quickly chase after other gods multiply their sorrows. I will not pour out their sacrificial offerings of blood or use my lips to speak their names. 
Psalms16:5 The Lord is my inheritance and my cup. You are the one who determines my destiny. 
Psalms16:6 Your boundary lines mark out pleasant places for me. Indeed, my inheritance is something beautiful." 
Psalms16:7 I will praise the Lord, who advises me. My conscience warns me at night." 
Psalms16:8 I always keep the Lord in front of me. When he is by my side, I cannot be moved." 
Psalms16:9 That is why my heart is glad and my soul rejoices. My body rests securely 
Psalms16:10 because you do not abandon my soul to the grave or allow your holy one to decay. 
Psalms16:11 You make the path of life known to me. Complete joy is in your presence. Pleasures are by your side forever. 
Psalms17:1 A prayer by David. Hear my plea for justice, O Lord. Pay attention to my cry. Open your ears to my prayer, {which comes} from lips free from deceit." 
Psalms17:2 Let the verdict of my innocence come directly from you. Let your eyes observe what is fair. 
Psalms17:3 You have probed my heart. You have confronted me at night. You have tested me like silver, but you found nothing wrong. I have determined that my mouth will not sin." 
Psalms17:4 I have avoided cruelty because of your word. In spite of what others have done," 
Psalms17:5 my steps have remained firmly in your paths. My feet have not slipped. 
Psalms17:6 I have called on you because you answer me, O God. Turn your ear toward me. Hear what I have to say." 
Psalms17:7 Reveal your miraculous deeds of mercy, O Savior of those who find refuge by your side from those who attack them." 
Psalms17:8 Guard me as if I were the pupil in your eye. Hide me in the shadow of your wings. 
Psalms17:9 Hide me from wicked people who violently attack me, from my deadly enemies who surround me." 
Psalms17:10 They have shut out all feeling. Their mouths have spoken arrogantly. 
Psalms17:11 They have tracked me down. They have surrounded me. They have focused their attention on throwing me to the ground. 
Psalms17:12 Each one of them is like a lion eager to tear {its prey} apart and like a young lion crouching in hiding places. 
Psalms17:13 Arise, O Lord; confront them! Bring them to their knees! With your sword rescue my life from wicked people." 
Psalms17:14 With your power rescue me from mortals, O Lord, from mortals who enjoy their inheritance only in this life. You fill their bellies with your treasure. Their children are satisfied {with it}, and they leave what remains to their children." 
Psalms17:15 I will see your face when I am declared innocent. When I wake up, I will be satisfied {with seeing} you." 
Psalms18:1 For the choir director; by David, the servant of the Lord. He sang this song to the Lord when the Lord rescued him from all his enemies, especially from Saul. He said, I love you, O Lord, my strength." 
Psalms18:2 The Lord is my rock and my fortress and my Savior, my God, my rock in whom I take refuge, my shield, and the strength of my salvation, my stronghold." 
Psalms18:3 The Lord should be praised. I called on him, and I was saved from my enemies." 
Psalms18:4 The ropes of death had become tangled around me. The torrents of destruction had overwhelmed me. 
Psalms18:5 The ropes of the grave had surrounded me. The clutches of death had confronted me. 
Psalms18:6 I called on the Lord in my distress. I cried to my God for help. He heard my voice from his temple, and my cry for help reached his ears." 
Psalms18:7 Then the earth shook and quaked. Even the foundations of the mountains trembled. They shook violently because he was angry. 
Psalms18:8 Smoke went up from his nostrils, and a raging fire came out of his mouth. Glowing coals flared up from it." 
Psalms18:9 He spread apart the heavens and came down with a dark cloud under his feet. 
Psalms18:10 He rode on one of the angels as he flew, and he soared on the wings of the wind." 
Psalms18:11 He made the darkness his hiding place, the dark rain clouds his covering." 
Psalms18:12 Out of the brightness in front of him, those rain clouds passed by with hailstones and lightning." 
Psalms18:13 The Lord thundered in the heavens. The Most High made his voice heard with hailstones and lightning. 
Psalms18:14 He shot his arrows and scattered them. He flashed streaks of lightning and threw them into confusion. 
Psalms18:15 Then the ocean floor could be seen. The foundations of the earth were laid bare at your stern warning, O Lord, at the blast of the breath from your nostrils." 
Psalms18:16 He reached down from high above and took hold of me. He pulled me out of the raging water. 
Psalms18:17 He rescued me from my strong enemy and from those who hated me, because they were too strong for me." 
Psalms18:18 On the day when I faced disaster, they confronted me, but the Lord came to my defense." 
Psalms18:19 He brought me out to a wide-open place. He rescued me because he was pleased with me. 
Psalms18:20 The Lord rewarded me because of my righteousness, because my hands are clean. He paid me back" 
Psalms18:21 because I have kept the ways of the Lord and I have not wickedly turned away from my God," 
Psalms18:22 because all his judgments are in front of me and I have not turned away from his laws. 
Psalms18:23 I was innocent as far as he was concerned. I have kept myself from guilt. 
Psalms18:24 The Lord paid me back because of my righteousness, because he can see that my hands are clean." 
Psalms18:25 {In dealing} with faithful people you are faithful, with innocent people you are innocent," 
Psalms18:26 with pure people you are pure. {In dealing} with devious people you are clever. 
Psalms18:27 You save humble people, but you bring down a conceited look." 
Psalms18:28 O Lord, you light my lamp. My God turns my darkness into light." 
Psalms18:29 With you I can attack a line of soldiers. With my God I can break through barricades. 
Psalms18:30 God's way is perfect! The promise of the Lord has proven to be true. He is a shield to all those who take refuge in him. 
Psalms18:31 Who is God but the Lord? Who is a rock except our God? 
Psalms18:32 God arms me with strength and makes my way perfect. 
Psalms18:33 He makes my feet like those of a deer and gives me sure footing on high places. 
Psalms18:34 He trains my hands for battle so that my arms can bend an {archer's} bow of bronze. 
Psalms18:35 You have given me the shield of your salvation. Your right hand supports me. Your gentleness makes me great. 
Psalms18:36 You make a wide path for me to walk on so that my feet do not slip. 
Psalms18:37 I chased my enemies and caught up with them. I did not return until I had ended their lives. 
Psalms18:38 I wounded them so badly that they were unable to get up. They fell under my feet. 
Psalms18:39 You armed me with strength for battle. You made my opponents bow at my feet. 
Psalms18:40 You made my enemies turn their backs to me, and I destroyed those who hated me." 
Psalms18:41 They cried out for help, but there was no one to save them. They cried out to the Lord, but he did not answer them." 
Psalms18:42 I beat them into a powder as fine as the dust blown by the wind. I threw them out as though they were dirt on the streets. 
Psalms18:43 You rescued me from my conflicts with the people. You made me the leader of nations. A people I did not know will serve me: 
Psalms18:44 As soon as they hear of me, they will obey me. Foreigners will cringe in front of me." 
Psalms18:45 Foreigners will lose heart, and they will tremble when they come out of their fortifications." 
Psalms18:46 The Lord lives! Thanks be to my rock! May God my Savior be honored. 
Psalms18:47 God gives me vengeance! He brings people under my authority. 
Psalms18:48 He saves me from my enemies. You lift me up above my opponents. You rescue me from violent people. 
Psalms18:49 That is why I will give thanks to you, O Lord, among the nations and make music to praise your name." 
Psalms18:50 He gives great victories to his king. He shows mercy to his anointed, to David, and to his descendant forever." 
Psalms19:1 For the choir director; a psalm by David. The heavens declare the glory of God, and the sky displays what his hands have made." 
Psalms19:2 One day tells a story to the next. One night shares knowledge with the next 
Psalms19:3 without talking, without words, without their voices being heard." 
Psalms19:4 {Yet,} their sound has gone out into the entire world, their message to the ends of the earth. He has set up a tent in the heavens for the sun," 
Psalms19:5 which comes out of its chamber like a bridegroom. Like a champion, it is eager to run its course." 
Psalms19:6 It rises from one end of the heavens. It circles around to the other. Nothing is hidden from its heat. 
Psalms19:7 The teachings of the Lord are perfect. They renew the soul. The testimony of the Lord is dependable. It makes gullible people wise. 
Psalms19:8 The instructions of the Lord are correct. They make the heart rejoice. The command of the Lord is radiant. It makes the eyes shine. 
Psalms19:9 The fear of the Lord is pure. It endures forever. The decisions of the Lord are true. They are completely fair. 
Psalms19:10 They are more desirable than gold, even the finest gold. They are sweeter than honey, even the drippings from a honeycomb." 
Psalms19:11 As your servant I am warned by them. There is a great reward in following them. 
Psalms19:12 Who can notice every mistake? Forgive my hidden faults. 
Psalms19:13 Keep me from sinning. Do not let anyone gain control over me. Then I will be blameless, and I will be free from any great offense." 
Psalms19:14 May the words from my mouth and the thoughts from my heart be acceptable to you, O Lord, my rock and my defender." 
Psalms20:1 For the choir director; a psalm by David. The Lord will answer you in times of trouble. The name of the God of Jacob will protect you. 
Psalms20:2 He will send you help from his holy place and support you from Zion. 
Psalms20:3 He will remember all your grain offerings and look with favor on your burnt offerings. Selah 
Psalms20:4 He will give you your heart's desire and carry out all your plans. 
Psalms20:5 We will joyfully sing about your victory. We will wave our flags in the name of our God. The Lord will fulfill all your requests. 
Psalms20:6 Now I know that the Lord will give victory to his anointed king. He will answer him from his holy heaven with mighty deeds of his powerful hand. 
Psalms20:7 Some {rely} on chariots and others on horses, but we will boast in the name of the Lord our God." 
Psalms20:8 They will sink to their knees and fall, but we will rise and stand firm." 
Psalms20:9 Give victory to the king, O Lord. Answer us when we call." 
Psalms21:1 For the choir director; a psalm by David. The king finds joy in your strength, O Lord. What great joy he has in your victory!" 
Psalms21:2 You gave him his heart's desire. You did not refuse the prayer from his lips. Selah 
Psalms21:3 You welcomed him with the blessings of good things and set a crown of fine gold on his head. 
Psalms21:4 He asked you for life. You gave him a long life, forever and ever." 
Psalms21:5 Because of your victory his glory is great. You place splendor and majesty on him. 
Psalms21:6 Yes, you made him a blessing forever. You made him glad with the joy of your presence." 
Psalms21:7 Indeed, the king trusts the Lord, and through the mercy of the Most High, he will not be moved." 
Psalms21:8 Your hand will discover all your enemies. Your powerful hand will find all who hate you. 
Psalms21:9 When you appear, you will make them {burn} like a blazing furnace. The Lord will swallow them up in his anger. Fire will devour them." 
Psalms21:10 You will destroy their children from the earth and their offspring from among Adam's descendants. 
Psalms21:11 Although they scheme and plan evil against you, they will not succeed." 
Psalms21:12 They turn their backs {and flee} because you aim your bow at their faces. 
Psalms21:13 Arise, O Lord, in your strength. We will sing and make music to praise your power." 
Psalms22:1 For the choir director; according to ayyeleth hashachar; a psalm by David. My God, my God, why have you abandoned me? Why are you so far away from helping me, so far away from the words of my groaning?" 
Psalms22:2 My God, I cry out by day, but you do not answer— also at night, but I find no rest." 
Psalms22:3 Yet, you are holy, enthroned on the praises of Israel." 
Psalms22:4 Our ancestors trusted you. They trusted, and you rescued them." 
Psalms22:5 They cried to you and were saved. They trusted you and were never disappointed. 
Psalms22:6 Yet, I am a worm and not a man. I am scorned by humanity and despised by people." 
Psalms22:7 All who see me make fun of me. Insults pour from their mouths. They shake their heads and say," 
Psalms22:8 Put yourself in the Lord's hands. Let the Lord save him! Let God rescue him since he is pleased with him! 
Psalms22:9 Indeed, you are the one who brought me out of the womb, the one who made me feel safe at my mother's breasts." 
Psalms22:10 I was placed in your care from birth. From my mother's womb you have been my God. 
Psalms22:11 Do not be so far away from me. Trouble is near, and there is no one to help." 
Psalms22:12 Many bulls have surrounded me. Strong bulls from Bashan have encircled me. 
Psalms22:13 They have opened their mouths to attack me like ferocious, roaring lions." 
Psalms22:14 I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint. My heart is like wax. It has melted within me." 
Psalms22:15 My strength is dried up like pieces of broken pottery. My tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth. You lay me down in the dust of death. 
Psalms22:16 Dogs have surrounded me. A mob has encircled me. They have pierced my hands and feet. 
Psalms22:17 I can count all my bones. People stare. They gloat over me. 
Psalms22:18 They divide my clothes among themselves. They throw dice for my clothing. 
Psalms22:19 Do not be so far away, O Lord. Come quickly to help me, O my strength." 
Psalms22:20 Rescue my soul from the sword, my life from vicious dogs." 
Psalms22:21 Save me from the mouth of the lion and from the horns of wild oxen. You have answered me. 
Psalms22:22 I will tell my people about your name. I will praise you within the congregation. 
Psalms22:23 All who fear the Lord, praise him! All you descendants of Jacob, glorify him! Stand in awe of him, all you descendants of Israel." 
Psalms22:24 The Lord has not despised or been disgusted with the plight of the oppressed one. He has not hidden his face from that person. The Lord heard when that oppressed person cried out to him for help. 
Psalms22:25 My praise comes from you while I am among those assembled for worship. I will fulfill my vows in the presence of those who fear the Lord. 
Psalms22:26 Oppressed people will eat until they are full. Those who look to the Lord will praise him. May you live forever. 
Psalms22:27 All the ends of the earth will remember and return to the Lord. All the families from all the nations will worship you 
Psalms22:28 because the kingdom belongs to the Lord and he rules the nations. 
Psalms22:29 All prosperous people on earth will eat and worship. All those who go down to the dust will kneel in front of him, even those who are barely alive." 
Psalms22:30 There will be descendants who serve him, a generation that will be told about the Lord." 
Psalms22:31 They will tell people yet to be born about his righteousness— that he has finished it. 
Psalms23:1 A psalm by David. The Lord is my shepherd. I am never in need. 
Psalms23:2 He makes me lie down in green pastures. He leads me beside peaceful waters. 
Psalms23:3 He renews my soul. He guides me along the paths of righteousness for the sake of his name. 
Psalms23:4 Even though I walk through the dark valley of death, because you are with me, I fear no harm. Your rod and your staff give me courage." 
Psalms23:5 You prepare a banquet for me while my enemies watch. You anoint my head with oil. My cup overflows. 
Psalms23:6 Certainly, goodness and mercy will stay close to me all the days of my life, and I will remain in the Lord's house for days without end." 
Psalms24:1 A psalm by David. The earth and everything it contains are the Lord's. The world and all who live in it are his. 
Psalms24:2 He laid its foundation on the seas and set it firmly on the rivers. 
Psalms24:3 Who may go up the Lord's mountain? Who may stand in his holy place? 
Psalms24:4 {The one who} has clean hands and a pure heart and does not long for what is false or lie when he is under oath. 
Psalms24:5 {This person} will receive a blessing from the Lord and righteousness from God, his savior." 
Psalms24:6 This is the person who seeks him, who searches for the face of the God of Jacob. Selah" 
Psalms24:7 Lift your heads, you gates. Be lifted, you ancient doors, so that the king of glory may come in." 
Psalms24:8 Who is this king of glory? The Lord, strong and mighty! The Lord, heroic in battle!" 
Psalms24:9 Lift your heads, you gates. Be lifted, you ancient doors, so that the king of glory may come in." 
Psalms24:10 Who, then, is this king of glory? The Lord of Armies is the king of glory! Selah" 
Psalms25:1 By David. To you, O Lord, I lift my soul." 
Psalms25:2 I trust you, O my God. Do not let me be put to shame. Do not let my enemies triumph over me." 
Psalms25:3 No one who waits for you will ever be put to shame, but all who are unfaithful will be put to shame." 
Psalms25:4 Make your ways known to me, O Lord, and teach me your paths." 
Psalms25:5 Lead me in your truth and teach me because you are God, my savior. I wait all day long for you." 
Psalms25:6 Remember, O Lord, your compassionate and merciful deeds. They have existed from eternity." 
Psalms25:7 Do not remember the sins of my youth or my rebellious ways. Remember me, O Lord, in keeping with your mercy and your goodness." 
Psalms25:8 The Lord is good and decent. That is why he teaches sinners the way they should live. 
Psalms25:9 He leads humble people to do what is right, and he teaches them his way." 
Psalms25:10 Every path of the Lord is {one of} mercy and truth for those who cling to his promise and written instructions. 
Psalms25:11 For the sake of your name, O Lord, remove my guilt, because it is great." 
Psalms25:12 Who, then, is this person that fears the Lord? He is the one whom the Lord will teach which path to choose." 
Psalms25:13 He will enjoy good things in life, and his descendants will inherit the land." 
Psalms25:14 The Lord advises those who fear him. He reveals to them the intent of his promise. 
Psalms25:15 My eyes are always on the Lord. He removes my feet from traps. 
Psalms25:16 Turn to me, and have pity on me. I am lonely and oppressed." 
Psalms25:17 Relieve my troubled heart, and bring me out of my distress." 
Psalms25:18 Look at my misery and suffering, and forgive all my sins." 
Psalms25:19 See how my enemies have increased in number, how they have hated me with vicious hatred!" 
Psalms25:20 Protect my life, and rescue me! Do not let me be put to shame. I have taken refuge in you." 
Psalms25:21 Integrity and honesty will protect me because I wait for you. 
Psalms25:22 Rescue Israel, O God, from all its troubles!" 
Psalms26:1 By David. Judge me favorably, O Lord, because I have walked with integrity and I have trusted you without wavering." 
Psalms26:2 Examine me, O Lord, and test me. Look closely into my heart and mind." 
Psalms26:3 I see your mercy in front of me. I walk in the light of your truth. 
Psalms26:4 I did not sit with liars, and I will not be found among hypocrites." 
Psalms26:5 I have hated the mob of evildoers and will not sit with wicked people. 
Psalms26:6 I will wash my hands in innocence. I will walk around your altar, O Lord," 
Psalms26:7 so that I may loudly sing a hymn of thanksgiving and tell about all your miracles. 
Psalms26:8 O Lord, I love the house where you live, the place where your glory dwells." 
Psalms26:9 Do not sweep away my soul along with hardened sinners or my life along with bloodthirsty people. 
Psalms26:10 Evil schemes are in their hands. Their right hands are full of bribes. 
Psalms26:11 But I walk with integrity. Rescue me, and have pity on me." 
Psalms26:12 My feet stand on level ground. I will praise the Lord with the choirs in worship. 
Psalms27:1 By David. The Lord is my light and my salvation. Who is there to fear? The Lord is my life's fortress. Who is there to be afraid of? 
Psalms27:2 Evildoers closed in on me to tear me to pieces. My opponents and enemies stumbled and fell. 
Psalms27:3 Even though an army sets up camp against me, my heart will not be afraid. Even though a war breaks out against me, I will still have confidence {in the Lord}." 
Psalms27:4 I have asked one thing from the Lord. This I will seek: to remain in the Lord's house all the days of my life in order to gaze at the Lord's beauty and to search for an answer in his temple. 
Psalms27:5 He hides me in his shelter when there is trouble. He keeps me hidden in his tent. He sets me high on a rock. 
Psalms27:6 Now my head will be raised above my enemies who surround me. I will offer sacrifices with shouts of joy in his tent. I will sing and make music to praise the Lord. 
Psalms27:7 Hear, O Lord, when I cry aloud. Have pity on me, and answer me." 
Psalms27:8 {When you said,} ""Seek my face,"" my heart said to you, ""O Lord, I will seek your face.""" 
Psalms27:9 Do not hide your face from me. Do not angrily turn me away. You have been my help. Do not leave me! Do not abandon me, O God, my savior!" 
Psalms27:10 Even if my father and mother abandon me, the Lord will take care of me." 
Psalms27:11 Teach me your way, O Lord. Lead me on a level path because I have enemies who spy on me." 
Psalms27:12 Do not surrender me to the will of my opponents. False witnesses have risen against me. They breathe out violence. 
Psalms27:13 I believe that I will see the goodness of the Lord in this world of the living. 
Psalms27:14 Wait with hope for the Lord. Be strong, and let your heart be courageous. Yes, wait with hope for the Lord." 
Psalms28:1 By David. O Lord, I call to you. O my rock, do not turn a deaf ear to me. If you remain silent, I will be like those who go into the pit." 
Psalms28:2 Hear my prayer for mercy when I call to you for help, when I lift my hands toward your most holy place." 
Psalms28:3 Do not drag me away with wicked people, with troublemakers who speak of peace with their neighbors but have evil in their hearts." 
Psalms28:4 Pay them back for what they have done, for their evil deeds. Pay them back for what their hands have done, and give them what they deserve." 
Psalms28:5 The Lord will tear them down and never build them up again, because they never consider what he has done or what his hands have made." 
Psalms28:6 Thank the Lord! He has heard my prayer for mercy! 
Psalms28:7 The Lord is my strength and my shield. My heart trusted him, so I received help. My heart is triumphant; I give thanks to him with my song." 
Psalms28:8 The Lord is the strength of his people and a fortress for the victory of his Messiah. 
Psalms28:9 Save your people, and bless those who belong to you. Be their shepherd, and carry them forever."

Psalms29:2 Give to the Lord the glory his name deserves. Worship the Lord in {his} holy splendor. 
Psalms29:3 The voice of the Lord rolls over the water. The God of glory thunders. The Lord shouts over raging water. 
Psalms29:4 The voice of the Lord is powerful. The voice of the Lord is majestic. 
Psalms29:5 The voice of the Lord breaks the cedars. The Lord splinters the cedars of Lebanon. 
Psalms29:6 He makes Lebanon skip along like a calf and Mount Sirion like a wild ox. 
Psalms29:7 The voice of the Lord strikes with flashes of lightning. 
Psalms29:8 The voice of the Lord makes the wilderness tremble. The Lord makes the wilderness of Kadesh tremble. 
Psalms29:9 The voice of the Lord splits the oaks and strips {the trees of} the forests bare. Everyone in his temple is saying, ""Glory!""" 
Psalms29:10 The Lord sat enthroned over the flood. The Lord sits enthroned as king forever. 
Psalms29:11 The Lord will give power to his people. The Lord will bless his people with peace. 
Psalms30:1 A psalm by David sung at the dedication of the temple. I will honor you highly, O Lord, because you have pulled me out {of the pit} and have not let my enemies rejoice over me." 
Psalms30:2 O Lord my God, I cried out to you for help, and you healed me." 
Psalms30:3 O Lord, you brought me up from the grave. You called me back to life from among those who had gone into the pit." 
Psalms30:4 Make music to praise the Lord, you faithful people who belong to him. Remember his holiness by giving thanks." 
Psalms30:5 His anger lasts only a moment. His favor lasts a lifetime. Weeping may last for the night, but there is a song of joy in the morning." 
Psalms30:6 When all was well with me, I said, ""I will never be shaken.""" 
Psalms30:7 O Lord, by your favor you have made my mountain stand firm. When you hid your face, I was terrified." 
Psalms30:8 I will cry out to you, O Lord. I will plead to the Lord for mercy:" 
Psalms30:9 How will you profit if my blood is shed, if I go into the pit? Will the dust {of my body} give thanks to you? Will it tell about your truth?""" 
Psalms30:10 Hear, O Lord, and have pity on me! O Lord, be my helper!" 
Psalms30:11 You have changed my sobbing into dancing. You have removed my sackcloth and clothed me with joy 
Psalms30:12 so that my soul may praise you with music and not be silent. O Lord my God, I will give thanks to you forever." 
Psalms31:1 For the choir director; a psalm by David. I have taken refuge in you, O Lord. Never let me be put to shame. Save me because of your righteousness." 
Psalms31:2 Turn your ear toward me. Rescue me quickly. Be a rock of refuge for me, a strong fortress to save me." 
Psalms31:3 Indeed, you are my rock and my fortress. For the sake of your name, lead me and guide me." 
Psalms31:4 You are my refuge, so pull me out of the net that they have secretly laid for me." 
Psalms31:5 Into your hands I entrust my spirit. You have rescued me, O Lord, God of truth." 
Psalms31:6 I hate those who cling to false gods, but I trust the Lord." 
Psalms31:7 I will rejoice and be glad because of your mercy. You have seen my misery. You have known the troubles in my soul. 
Psalms31:8 You have not handed me over to the enemy. You have set my feet in a place where I can move freely. 
Psalms31:9 Have pity on me, O Lord, because I am in distress. My eyes, my soul, and my body waste away from grief." 
Psalms31:10 My life is exhausted from sorrow, my years from groaning. My strength staggers under {the weight of} my guilt, and my bones waste away." 
Psalms31:11 I have become a disgrace because of all my opponents. I have become someone dreaded by my friends, even by my neighbors. Those who see me on the street run away from me." 
Psalms31:12 I have faded from memory as if I were dead and have become like a piece of broken pottery. 
Psalms31:13 I have heard the whispering of many people— terror on every side— while they made plans together against me. They were plotting to take my life. 
Psalms31:14 I trust you, O Lord. I said, ""You are my God.""" 
Psalms31:15 My future is in your hands. Rescue me from my enemies, from those who persecute me." 
Psalms31:16 Smile on me. Save me with your mercy. 
Psalms31:17 O Lord, I have called on you, so do not let me be put to shame, Let wicked people be put to shame. Let them be silent in the grave." 
Psalms31:18 Let {their} lying lips be speechless, since they speak against righteous people with arrogance and contempt." 
Psalms31:19 Your kindness is so great! You reserve it for those who fear you. Adam's descendants watch as you show it to those who take refuge in you. 
Psalms31:20 You hide them in the secret place of your presence from those who scheme against them. You keep them in a shelter, safe from quarrelsome tongues." 
Psalms31:21 Thank the Lord! He has shown me the miracle of his mercy in a city under attack. 
Psalms31:22 When I was panic-stricken, I said, ""I have been cut off from your sight."" But you heard my pleas for mercy when I cried out to you for help." 
Psalms31:23 Love the Lord, all you godly ones! The Lord protects faithful people, but he pays back in full those who act arrogantly." 
Psalms31:24 Be strong, all who wait with hope for the Lord, and let your heart be courageous." 
Psalms32:1 A psalm by David; a maskil. Blessed is the person whose disobedience is forgiven and whose sin is pardoned. 
Psalms32:2 Blessed is the person whom the Lord no longer accuses of sin and who has no deceitful thoughts. 
Psalms32:3 When I kept silent {about my sins}, my bones began to weaken because of my groaning all day long." 
Psalms32:4 Day and night your hand lay heavily on me. My strength shriveled in the summer heat. Selah 
Psalms32:5 I made my sins known to you, and I did not cover up my guilt. I decided to confess them to you, O Lord. Then you forgave all my sins. Selah" 
Psalms32:6 For this reason let all godly people pray to you when you may be found. Then raging floodwater will not reach them. 
Psalms32:7 You are my hiding place. You protect me from trouble. You surround me with joyous songs of salvation. Selah 
Psalms32:8 {The Lord says,} ""I will instruct you. I will teach you the way that you should go. I will advise you as my eyes watch over you." 
Psalms32:9 Don't be stubborn like a horse or mule. {They need} a bit and bridle in their mouth to restrain them, or they will not come near you.""" 
Psalms32:10 Many heartaches await wicked people, but mercy surrounds those who trust the Lord." 
Psalms32:11 Be glad and find joy in the Lord, you righteous people. Sing with joy, all whose motives are decent." 
Psalms33:1 Joyfully sing to the Lord, you righteous people. Praising {the Lord} is proper for decent people." 
Psalms33:2 Give thanks with a lyre to the Lord. Make music for him on a ten-stringed harp. 
Psalms33:3 Sing a new song to him. Play beautifully and joyfully on stringed instruments. 
Psalms33:4 The word of the Lord is correct, and everything he does is trustworthy." 
Psalms33:5 The Lord loves righteousness and justice. His mercy fills the earth. 
Psalms33:6 The heavens were made by the word of the Lord and all the stars by the breath of his mouth. 
Psalms33:7 He gathers the water in the sea like a dam and puts the oceans in his storehouses. 
Psalms33:8 Let all the earth fear the Lord. Let all who live in the world stand in awe of him. 
Psalms33:9 He spoke, and it came into being. He gave the order, and there it stood." 
Psalms33:10 The Lord blocks the plans of the nations. He frustrates the schemes of the people of the world. 
Psalms33:11 The Lord's plan stands firm forever. His thoughts stand firm in every generation. 
Psalms33:12 Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord. Blessed are the people he has chosen as his own. 
Psalms33:13 The Lord looks down from heaven. He sees all of Adam's descendants. 
Psalms33:14 From the place where he sits enthroned, he looks down upon all who live on earth." 
Psalms33:15 The one who formed their hearts understands everything they do. 
Psalms33:16 No king achieves a victory with a large army. No warrior rescues himself by his own great strength. 
Psalms33:17 Horses are not a guarantee for victory. Their great strength cannot help someone escape. 
Psalms33:18 The Lord's eyes are on those who fear him, on those who wait with hope for his mercy" 
Psalms33:19 to rescue their souls from death and keep them alive during a famine. 
Psalms33:20 We wait for the Lord. He is our help and our shield. 
Psalms33:21 In him our hearts find joy. In his holy name we trust. 
Psalms33:22 Let your mercy rest on us, O Lord, since we wait with hope for you." 
Psalms34:1 By David when he pretended to be insane in the presence of Abimelech; Abimelech threw him out, so David left. I will thank the Lord at all times. My mouth will always praise him." 
Psalms34:2 My soul will boast about the Lord. Those who are oppressed will hear it and rejoice. 
Psalms34:3 Praise the Lord's greatness with me. Let us highly honor his name together. 
Psalms34:4 I went to the Lord for help. He answered me and rescued me from all my fears. 
Psalms34:5 All who look to him will be radiant. Their faces will never be covered with shame. 
Psalms34:6 Here is a poor man who called out. The Lord heard him and saved him from all his troubles. 
Psalms34:7 The Messenger of the Lord camps around those who fear him, and he rescues them." 
Psalms34:8 Taste and see that the Lord is good. Blessed is the person who takes refuge in him. 
Psalms34:9 Fear the Lord, you holy people who belong to him. Those who fear him are never in need." 
Psalms34:10 Young lions go hungry and may starve, but those who seek the Lord's help have all the good things they need." 
Psalms34:11 Come, children, listen to me. I will teach you the fear of the Lord." 
Psalms34:12 Which of you wants a full life? Who would like to live long enough to enjoy good things? 
Psalms34:13 Keep your tongue from saying evil things and your lips from speaking deceitful things. 
Psalms34:14 Turn away from evil, and do good. Seek peace, and pursue it!" 
Psalms34:15 The Lord's eyes are on righteous people. His ears hear their cry for help. 
Psalms34:16 The Lord confronts those who do evil in order to wipe out all memory of them from the earth. 
Psalms34:17 {Righteous people} cry out. The Lord hears and rescues them from all their troubles. 
Psalms34:18 The Lord is near to those whose hearts are humble. He saves those whose spirits are crushed. 
Psalms34:19 The righteous person has many troubles, but the Lord rescues him from all of them." 
Psalms34:20 The Lord guards all of his bones. Not one of them is broken. 
Psalms34:21 Evil will kill wicked people, and those who hate righteous people will be condemned." 
Psalms34:22 The Lord protects the souls of his servants. All who take refuge in him will never be condemned. 
Psalms35:1 By David. O Lord, attack those who attack me. Fight against those who fight against me." 
Psalms35:2 Use your shields, {both} small and large. Arise to help me." 
Psalms35:3 Hold your spear to block the way of those who pursue me. Say to my soul, ""I am your savior.""" 
Psalms35:4 Let those who seek my life be put to shame and disgraced. Let those who plan my downfall be turned back in confusion. 
Psalms35:5 Let them be like husks blown by the wind as the Messenger of the Lord chases them. 
Psalms35:6 Let their path be dark and slippery as the Messenger of the Lord pursues them. 
Psalms35:7 For no reason they hid their net in a pit. For no reason they dug the pit {to trap me}. 
Psalms35:8 Let destruction surprise them. Let the net that they hid catch them. Let them fall into their own pit and be destroyed. 
Psalms35:9 My soul will find joy in the Lord and be joyful about his salvation. 
Psalms35:10 All my bones will say, ""O Lord, who can compare with you? You rescue the weak person from the one who is too strong for him and weak and needy people from the one who robs them.""" 
Psalms35:11 Malicious people bring charges against me. They ask me things I know nothing about. 
Psalms35:12 I am devastated because they pay me back with evil instead of good. 
Psalms35:13 But when they were sick, I wore sackcloth. I humbled myself with fasting. When my prayer returned unanswered," 
Psalms35:14 I walked around as if I were mourning for my friend or my brother. I was bent over as if I were mourning for my mother. 
Psalms35:15 Yet, when I stumbled, they rejoiced and gathered together. They gathered together against me. Unknown attackers tore me apart without stopping." 
Psalms35:16 With crude and abusive mockers, they grit their teeth at me." 
Psalms35:17 O Lord, how long will you look on? Rescue me from their attacks. Rescue my precious life from the lions." 
Psalms35:18 I will give you thanks in a large gathering. I will praise you in a crowd {of worshipers}. 
Psalms35:19 Do not let my treacherous enemies gloat over me. Do not let those who hate me for no reason wink {at me}. 
Psalms35:20 They do not talk about peace. Instead, they scheme against the peaceful people in the land." 
Psalms35:21 They open their big mouths and say about me, ""Aha! Aha! Our own eyes have seen it.""" 
Psalms35:22 You have seen it, O Lord. Do not remain silent. O Lord, do not be so far away from me." 
Psalms35:23 Wake up, and rise to my defense. Plead my case, O my God and my Lord." 
Psalms35:24 Judge me by your righteousness, O Lord my God. Do not let them gloat over me" 
Psalms35:25 or think, ""Aha, just what we wanted!"" Do not let them say, ""We have swallowed him up.""" 
Psalms35:26 Let those who gloat over my downfall be thoroughly put to shame and confused. Let those who promote themselves at my expense be clothed with shame and disgrace. 
Psalms35:27 Let those who are happy when I am declared innocent joyfully sing and rejoice. Let them continually say, ""The Lord is great. He is happy when his servant has peace.""" 
Psalms35:28 Then my tongue will tell about your righteousness, about your praise all day long." 
Psalms36:1 For the choir director; by David, the Lord's servant. There is an inspired truth about the wicked person who has rebellion in the depths of his heart: He is not terrified of God." 
Psalms36:2 He flatters himself and does not hate or {even} recognize his guilt. 
Psalms36:3 The words from his mouth are {nothing but} trouble and deception. He has stopped doing what is wise and good. 
Psalms36:4 He invents trouble while lying on his bed and chooses to go the wrong direction. He does not reject evil. 
Psalms36:5 O Lord, your mercy reaches to the heavens, your faithfulness to the skies." 
Psalms36:6 Your righteousness is like the mountains of God, your judgments like the deep ocean. You save people and animals, O Lord." 
Psalms36:7 Your mercy is so precious, O God, that Adam's descendants take refuge in the shadow of your wings." 
Psalms36:8 They are refreshed with the rich foods in your house, and you make them drink from the river of your pleasure." 
Psalms36:9 Indeed, the fountain of life is with you. In your light we see light." 
Psalms36:10 Continue to show your mercy to those who know you and your righteousness to those whose motives are decent. 
Psalms36:11 Do not let the feet of arrogant people step on me or the hands of wicked people push me away. 
Psalms36:12 Look at the troublemakers who have fallen. They have been pushed down and are unable to stand up again. 
Psalms37:1 By David. Do not be preoccupied with evildoers. Do not envy those who do wicked things. 
Psalms37:2 They will quickly dry up like grass and wither away like green plants. 
Psalms37:3 Trust the Lord, and do good things. Live in the land, and practice being faithful." 
Psalms37:4 Be happy with the Lord, and he will give you the desires of your heart." 
Psalms37:5 Entrust your ways to the Lord. Trust him, and he will act {on your behalf}." 
Psalms37:6 He will make your righteousness shine like a light, your just cause like the noonday sun." 
Psalms37:7 Surrender yourself to the Lord, and wait patiently for him. Do not be preoccupied with {an evildoer} who succeeds in his way when he carries out his schemes." 
Psalms37:8 Let go of anger, and leave rage behind. Do not be preoccupied. It only leads to evil." 
Psalms37:9 Evildoers will be cut off {from their inheritance}, but those who wait with hope for the Lord will inherit the land." 
Psalms37:10 In a little while a wicked person will vanish. Then you can carefully examine where he was, but there will be no trace of him." 
Psalms37:11 Oppressed people will inherit the land and will enjoy unlimited peace. 
Psalms37:12 The wicked person plots against a righteous one and grits his teeth at him. 
Psalms37:13 The Lord laughs at him because he has seen that his time is coming. 
Psalms37:14 Wicked people pull out their swords and bend their bows to kill oppressed and needy people, to slaughter those who are decent." 
Psalms37:15 {But} their own swords will pierce their hearts, and their bows will be broken." 
Psalms37:16 The little that the righteous person has is better than the wealth of many wicked people. 
Psalms37:17 The arms of wicked people will be broken, but the Lord continues to support righteous people." 
Psalms37:18 The Lord knows the daily {struggles} of innocent people. Their inheritance will last forever. 
Psalms37:19 They will not be put to shame in trying times. Even in times of famine they will be satisfied. 
Psalms37:20 But wicked people will disappear. The Lord's enemies will vanish like the best part of a meadow. They will vanish like smoke. 
Psalms37:21 A wicked person borrows, but he does not repay. A righteous person is generous and giving." 
Psalms37:22 Those who are blessed by him will inherit the land. Those who are cursed by him will be cut off. 
Psalms37:23 A person's steps are directed by the Lord, and the Lord delights in his way." 
Psalms37:24 When he falls, he will not be thrown down headfirst because the Lord holds on to his hand." 
Psalms37:25 I have been young, and now I am old, but I have never seen a righteous person abandoned or his descendants begging for food." 
Psalms37:26 He is always generous and lends freely. His descendants are a blessing. 
Psalms37:27 Avoid evil, do good, and live forever." 
Psalms37:28 The Lord loves justice, and he will not abandon his godly ones. They will be kept safe forever, but the descendants of wicked people will be cut off." 
Psalms37:29 Righteous people will inherit the land and live there permanently. 
Psalms37:30 The mouth of the righteous person reflects on wisdom. His tongue speaks what is fair. 
Psalms37:31 The teachings of his God are in his heart. His feet do not slip. 
Psalms37:32 The wicked person watches the righteous person and seeks to kill him. 
Psalms37:33 But the Lord will not abandon him to the wicked person's power or condemn him when he is brought to trial. 
Psalms37:34 Wait with hope for the Lord, and follow his path, and he will honor you by giving you the land. When wicked people are cut off, you will see it." 
Psalms37:35 I have seen a wicked person {acting like} a tyrant, spreading himself out like a large cedar tree." 
Psalms37:36 But he moved on, and now there is no trace of him. I searched for him, but he could not be found." 
Psalms37:37 Notice the innocent person, and look at the decent person, because the peacemaker has a future." 
Psalms37:38 But rebels will be completely destroyed. The future of wicked people will be cut off. 
Psalms37:39 The victory for righteous people comes from the Lord. He is their fortress in times of trouble. 
Psalms37:40 The Lord helps them and rescues them. He rescues them from wicked people. He saves them because they have taken refuge in him. 
Psalms38:1 A psalm by David; to be kept in mind. O Lord, do not angrily punish me or discipline me in your wrath." 
Psalms38:2 Your arrows have struck me. Your hand has struck me hard. 
Psalms38:3 No healthy spot is left on my body because of your rage. There is no peace in my bones because of my sin. 
Psalms38:4 My guilt has overwhelmed me. Like a heavy load, it is more than I can bear." 
Psalms38:5 My wounds smell rotten. They fester because of my stupidity. 
Psalms38:6 I am bent over and bowed down very low. All day I walk around in mourning. 
Psalms38:7 My insides are filled with burning pain, and no healthy spot is left on my body." 
Psalms38:8 I am numb and completely devastated. I roar because my heart's in turmoil. 
Psalms38:9 You know all my desires, O Lord, and my groaning has not been hidden from you." 
Psalms38:10 My heart is pounding. I have lost my strength. Even the light of my eyes has left me. 
Psalms38:11 My loved ones and my friends keep their distance and my relatives stand far away because of my sickness. 
Psalms38:12 Those who seek my life lay traps for me. Those who are out to harm me talk about ruining me. All day long they think of ways to deceive me. 
Psalms38:13 But I am like a person who cannot hear and like a person who cannot speak. 
Psalms38:14 I am like one who cannot hear and who can offer no arguments. 
Psalms38:15 But I wait with hope for you, O Lord. You will answer, O Lord, my God." 
Psalms38:16 I said, ""Do not let them gloat over me. When my foot slips, do not let them promote themselves at my expense.""" 
Psalms38:17 I am ready to fall. I am continually aware of my pain. 
Psalms38:18 I confess my guilt. My sin troubles me. 
Psalms38:19 My mortal enemies are growing stronger. Many hate me for no reason. 
Psalms38:20 They pay me back with evil instead of good, and they accuse me because I try to do what is good." 
Psalms38:21 Do not abandon me, O Lord. O my God, do not be so distant from me." 
Psalms38:22 Come quickly to help me, O Lord, my savior." 
Psalms39:1 For the choir director; for Jeduthun; a psalm by David. I said, ""I will watch my ways so that I do not sin with my tongue. I will bridle my mouth while wicked people are in my presence.""" 
Psalms39:2 I remained totally speechless. I kept silent, although it did me no good. While I was deep in thought, my pain grew worse." 
Psalms39:3 My heart burned like a fire flaring up within me. Then I spoke with my tongue: 
Psalms39:4 Teach me, O Lord, about the end of my life. Teach me about the number of days I have left so that I may know how temporary my life is." 
Psalms39:5 Indeed, you have made the length of my days {only} a few inches. My life span is nothing compared to yours. Certainly, everyone alive is like a whisper in the wind. Selah" 
Psalms39:6 Each person who walks around is like a shadow. They are busy for no reason. They accumulate riches without knowing who will get them. 
Psalms39:7 And now, Lord, what am I waiting for? My hope is in you!" 
Psalms39:8 Rescue me from all my rebellious acts. Do not disgrace me in front of godless fools. 
Psalms39:9 I remained speechless. I did not open my mouth because you are the one who has done this. 
Psalms39:10 Remove the sickness you laid upon me. My life is over because you struck me with your hand. 
Psalms39:11 With stern warnings you discipline people for their crimes. Like a moth you eat away at what is dear to them. Certainly, everyone is like a whisper in the wind. Selah" 
Psalms39:12 Listen to my prayer, O Lord. Open your ear to my cry for help. Do not be deaf to my tears, for I am a foreign resident with you, a stranger like all my ancestors." 
Psalms39:13 Look away from me so that I may smile again before I go away and am no more. 
Psalms40:1 For the choir director; a psalm by David. I waited patiently for the Lord. He turned to me and heard my cry for help. 
Psalms40:2 He pulled me out of a horrible pit, out of the mud and clay. He set my feet on a rock and made my steps secure." 
Psalms40:3 He placed a new song in my mouth, a song of praise to our God. Many will see this and worship. They will trust the Lord." 
Psalms40:4 Blessed is the person who places his confidence in the Lord and does not rely on arrogant people or those who follow lies. 
Psalms40:5 You have done many miraculous things, O Lord my God. You have made many wonderful plans for us. No one compares to you! I will tell others about your miracles, which are more than I can count." 
Psalms40:6 You were not pleased with sacrifices and offerings. You have dug out two ears for me. You did not ask for burnt offerings or sacrifices for sin. 
Psalms40:7 Then I said, ""I have come! (It is written about me in the scroll of the book.)" 
Psalms40:8 I am happy to do your will, O my God."" Your teachings are deep within me." 
Psalms40:9 I will announce the good news of righteousness among those assembled for worship. I will not close my lips. You know that, O Lord." 
Psalms40:10 I have not buried your righteousness deep in my heart. I have been outspoken about your faithfulness and your salvation. I have not hidden your mercy and your truth from those assembled for worship. 
Psalms40:11 Do not withhold your compassion from me, O Lord. May your mercy and your truth always protect me." 
Psalms40:12 Countless evils have surrounded me. My sins have caught up with me so that I can no longer see. They outnumber the hairs on my head. I have lost heart. 
Psalms40:13 O Lord, please rescue me! Come quickly to help me, O Lord!" 
Psalms40:14 Let all those who seek to end my life be confused and put to shame. Let those who want my downfall be turned back and disgraced. 
Psalms40:15 Let those who say to me, ""Aha! Aha!"" be stunned by their own shame." 
Psalms40:16 Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad because of you. Let those who love your salvation continually say, ""The Lord is great!""" 
Psalms40:17 But I am oppressed and needy. May the Lord think of me. You are my help and my savior. O my God, do not delay!" 
Psalms41:1 For the choir director; a psalm by David. Blessed is the one who has concern for helpless people. The Lord will rescue him in times of trouble. 
Psalms41:2 The Lord will protect him and keep him alive. He will be blessed in the land. Do not place him at the mercy of his enemies. 
Psalms41:3 The Lord will support him on his sickbed. You will restore this person to health when he is ill. 
Psalms41:4 I said, ""O Lord, have pity on me! Heal my soul because I have sinned against you.""" 
Psalms41:5 My enemies say terrible things about me: When will he die, and when will his family name disappear?""" 
Psalms41:6 When one of them comes to visit me, he speaks foolishly. His heart collects gossip. {Then} he leaves to tell others." 
Psalms41:7 Everyone who hates me whispers about me. They think evil things about me and say," 
Psalms41:8 A devilish disease has attached itself to him. He will never leave his sickbed. 
Psalms41:9 Even my closest friend whom I trusted, the one who ate my bread, has lifted his heel against me." 
Psalms41:10 Have pity on me, O Lord! Raise me up so that I can pay them back" 
Psalms41:11 and my enemy cannot shout in triumph over me. When you do this, I know that you are pleased with me." 
Psalms41:12 You defend my integrity, and you set me in your presence forever." 
Psalms41:13 Thank the Lord God of Israel through all eternity! Amen and amen! 
Psalms42:1 For the choir director; a maskil by Korah's descendants. As a deer longs for flowing streams, so my soul longs for you, O God." 
Psalms42:2 My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When may I come to see God's face?" 
Psalms42:3 My tears are my food day and night. People ask me all day long, ""Where is your God?""" 
Psalms42:4 I will remember these things as I pour out my soul: how I used to walk with the crowd and lead it in a procession to God's house. {I sang} songs of joy and thanksgiving while crowds of people celebrated a festival. 
Psalms42:5 Why are you discouraged, my soul? Why are you so restless? Put your hope in God, because I will still praise him. He is my savior and my God." 
Psalms42:6 My soul is discouraged. That is why I will remember you in the land of Jordan, on the peaks of Hermon, on Mount Mizar." 
Psalms42:7 One deep sea calls to another at the roar of your waterspouts. All the whitecaps on your waves have swept over me. 
Psalms42:8 The Lord commands his mercy during the day, and at night his song is with me— a prayer to the God of my life." 
Psalms42:9 I will ask God, my rock, ""Why have you forgotten me? Why must I walk around in mourning while the enemy oppresses me?""" 
Psalms42:10 With a shattering blow to my bones, my enemies taunt me. They ask me all day long, ""Where is your God?""" 
Psalms42:11 Why are you discouraged, my soul? Why are you so restless? Put your hope in God, because I will still praise him. He is my savior and my God." 
Psalms43:1 Judge me, O God, and plead my case against an ungodly nation. Rescue me from deceitful and unjust people." 
Psalms43:2 You are my fortress, O God! Why have you rejected me? Why must I walk around in mourning while the enemy oppresses me?" 
Psalms43:3 Send your light and your truth. Let them guide me. Let them bring me to your holy mountain and to your dwelling place. 
Psalms43:4 Then let me go to the altar of God, to God my {highest} joy, and I will give thanks to you on the lyre, O God, my God." 
Psalms43:5 Why are you discouraged, my soul? Why are you so restless? Put your hope in God, because I will still praise him. He is my savior and my God." 
Psalms44:1 For the choir director; a maskil by Korah's descendants. O God, we have heard it with our own ears. Our ancestors have told us about the miracle you performed in their day, in days long ago." 
Psalms44:2 By your power you forced nations {out of the land}, but you planted our ancestors {there}. You shattered many groups of people, but you set our ancestors free." 
Psalms44:3 It was not with their swords that they took possession of the land. They did not gain victory with their own strength. It was your right hand, your arm, and the light of your presence {that did it}, because you were pleased with them." 
Psalms44:4 You alone are my king, O God. You won those victories for Jacob." 
Psalms44:5 With you we can walk over our enemies. With your name we can trample those who attack us. 
Psalms44:6 I do not rely on my bow, and my sword will never save me." 
Psalms44:7 But you saved us from our enemies. You put to shame those who hate us. 
Psalms44:8 All day long we praise our God. We give thanks to you forever. Selah 
Psalms44:9 But now you have rejected and disgraced us. You do not even go along with our armies. 
Psalms44:10 You make us retreat from the enemy. Those who hate us rob us at will. 
Psalms44:11 You hand us over to be butchered like sheep and scatter us among the nations. 
Psalms44:12 You sell your people for almost nothing, and at that price you have gained nothing." 
Psalms44:13 You made us a disgrace to our neighbors and an object of ridicule and contempt to those around us. 
Psalms44:14 You made our {defeat} a proverb among the nations so that people shake their heads at us. 
Psalms44:15 All day long my disgrace is in front of me. Shame covers my face 
Psalms44:16 because of the words of those who insult and slander us, because of the presence of the enemy and the avenger." 
Psalms44:17 Although all of this happened to us, we never forgot you. We never ignored your promise." 
Psalms44:18 Our hearts never turned away. Our feet never left your path. 
Psalms44:19 Yet, you crushed us in a place for jackals and covered us with the shadow of death." 
Psalms44:20 If we forgot the name of our God or stretched out our hands to pray to another god," 
Psalms44:21 wouldn't God find out, since he knows the secrets in our hearts?" 
Psalms44:22 Indeed, we are being killed all day long because of you. We are thought of as sheep to be slaughtered." 
Psalms44:23 Wake up! Why are you sleeping, O Lord? Awake! Do not reject us forever!" 
Psalms44:24 Why do you hide your face? Why do you forget our suffering and misery? 
Psalms44:25 Our souls are bowing in the dust. Our bodies cling to the ground. 
Psalms44:26 Arise! Help us! Rescue us because of your mercy! 
Psalms45:1 For the choir director; according to shoshannim; a maskil by Korah's descendants; a love song. My heart is overflowing with good news. I will direct my song to the king. My tongue is a pen for a skillful writer. 
Psalms45:2 You are the most handsome of Adam's descendants. Grace is poured on your lips. That is why God has blessed you forever. 
Psalms45:3 O warrior, strap your sword to your side with your splendor and majesty." 
Psalms45:4 Ride on victoriously in your majesty for the cause of truth, humility, and righteousness. Let your right hand teach you awe-inspiring things." 
Psalms45:5 Your arrows are sharp in the heart of the king's enemies. Nations fall beneath you. 
Psalms45:6 Your throne, O God, is forever and ever. The scepter in your kingdom is a scepter for justice." 
Psalms45:7 You have loved what is right and hated what is wrong. That is why God, your God, has anointed you, rather than your companions, with the oil of joy." 
Psalms45:8 All your robes are {fragrant} with myrrh, aloes, and cassia. From ivory palaces the music of stringed instruments delights you." 
Psalms45:9 The daughters of kings are among your noble ladies. The queen takes her place at your right hand and wears gold from Ophir. 
Psalms45:10 Listen, daughter! Look closely! Turn your ear {toward me}. Forget your people, and forget your father's house." 
Psalms45:11 The king longs for your beauty. He is your Lord. Worship him. 
Psalms45:12 The people of Tyre, the richest people, want to win your favor with a gift." 
Psalms45:13 The daughter of the king is glorious inside {the palace}. Her dress is embroidered with gold. 
Psalms45:14 Wearing a colorful gown, she is brought to the king. Her bridesmaids follow her. They will be brought to you." 
Psalms45:15 With joy and delight they are brought in. They enter the palace of the king. 
Psalms45:16 Your sons will take the place of your father. You will make them princes over the whole earth. 
Psalms45:17 I will cause your name to be remembered throughout every generation. That is why the nations will give thanks to you forever and ever.

Psalms46:5 God is in that city. It cannot fall. God will help it at the break of dawn.

Psalms46:9 He puts an end to wars all over the earth. He breaks an archer's bow. He cuts spears in two. He burns chariots.

Psalms47:3 He brings people under our authority and {puts} nations under our feet. 
Psalms47:4 He chooses our inheritance for us, the pride of Jacob, whom he loved. Selah"

Psalms48:2 Its beautiful peak is the joy of the whole earth. Mount Zion is on the northern ridge. It is the city of the great king. 
Psalms48:3 God is in its palaces. He has proved that he is a stronghold. 
Psalms48:4 The kings have gathered. They marched together. 
Psalms48:5 {When} they saw {Mount Zion}, they were astonished. They were terrified and ran away in fear." 
Psalms48:6 Trembling seized them like the trembling that a woman experiences during labor. 
Psalms48:7 With the east wind you smash the ships of Tarshish. 
Psalms48:8 The things we had only heard about, we have now seen in the city of the Lord of Armies, in the city of our God. God makes Zion stand firm forever. Selah"

Psalms48:10 Like your name, O God, your praise {reaches} to the ends of the earth. Your right hand is filled with righteousness."

Psalms49:1 For the choir director; a psalm by Korah's descendants. Listen to this, all you people. Open your ears, all who live in the world—" 
Psalms49:2 common people and important ones, rich people and poor ones." 
Psalms49:3 My mouth will speak wise sayings, the insights I have carefully considered." 
Psalms49:4 I will turn my attention to a proverb. I will explain my riddle with the {music of} a lyre. 
Psalms49:5 Why should I be afraid in times of trouble, when slanderers surround me with evil?" 
Psalms49:6 They trust their riches and brag about their abundant wealth. 
Psalms49:7 No one can ever buy back another person or pay God a ransom for his life. 
Psalms49:8 The price to be paid for his soul is too costly. He must always give up 
Psalms49:9 in order to live forever and never see the pit. 
Psalms49:10 Indeed, one can see that wise people die, that foolish and stupid people meet the same end. They leave their riches to others." 
Psalms49:11 Although they named their lands after themselves, their graves have become their homes for ages to come, their dwelling places throughout every generation." 
Psalms49:12 But mortals will not continue here with what they treasure. They are like animals that die. 
Psalms49:13 This is the final outcome for fools and their followers who are delighted by what they say: Selah 
Psalms49:14 Like sheep, they are driven to hell with death as their shepherd. (Decent people will rule them in the morning.) Their forms will decay in the grave, far away from their comfortable homes." 
Psalms49:15 But God will buy me back from the power of hell because he will take me. Selah 
Psalms49:16 Do not be afraid when someone becomes rich, when the greatness of his house increases." 
Psalms49:17 He will not take anything with him when he dies. His greatness cannot follow him. 
Psalms49:18 Even though he blesses himself while he is alive (and they praise you when you do well for yourself)," 
Psalms49:19 he must join the generation of his ancestors, who will never see light {again}." 
Psalms49:20 Mortals, with what they treasure, still don't have understanding. They are like animals that die." 
Psalms50:1 A psalm by Asaph. The Lord, the only true God, has spoken. He has summoned the earth from where the sun rises to where it sets." 
Psalms50:2 God shines from Zion, the perfection of beauty." 
Psalms50:3 Our God will come and will not remain silent. A devouring fire is in front of him and a raging storm around him. 
Psalms50:4 He summons heaven and earth to judge his people: 
Psalms50:5 Gather around me, my godly people who have made a pledge to me through sacrifices.""" 
Psalms50:6 The heavens announce his righteousness because God is the judge. Selah 
Psalms50:7 Listen, my people, and I will speak. Listen, Israel, and I will testify against you: I am God, your God!" 
Psalms50:8 I am not criticizing you for your sacrifices or burnt offerings, which are always in front of me." 
Psalms50:9 {But} I will not accept {another} young bull from your household or a single male goat from your pens. 
Psalms50:10 Every creature in the forest, {even} the cattle on a thousand hills, is mine." 
Psalms50:11 I know every bird in the mountains. Everything that moves in the fields is mine. 
Psalms50:12 If I were hungry, I would not tell you, because the world and all that it contains are mine." 
Psalms50:13 Do I eat the meat of bulls or drink the blood of goats? 
Psalms50:14 Bring {your} thanks to God as a sacrifice, and keep your vows to the Most High." 
Psalms50:15 Call on me in times of trouble. I will rescue you, and you will honor me.""" 
Psalms50:16 But God says to wicked people, ""How dare you quote my decrees and mouth my promises!" 
Psalms50:17 You hate discipline. You toss my words behind you. 
Psalms50:18 When you see a thief, you want to make friends with him. You keep company with people who commit adultery." 
Psalms50:19 You let your mouth say anything evil. Your tongue plans deceit. 
Psalms50:20 You sit and talk against your own brother. You slander your own mother's son. 
Psalms50:21 When you did these things, I remained silent. {That} made you think I was like you. I will argue my point with you and lay it all out for you to see." 
Psalms50:22 Consider this, you people who forget God. Otherwise, I will tear you to pieces, and there will be no one left to rescue you." 
Psalms50:23 Whoever offers thanks as a sacrifice honors me. I will let everyone who continues in my way see the salvation that comes from God. 
Psalms51:1 For the choir director; a psalm by David when the prophet Nathan came to him after David's adultery with Bathsheba. Have pity on me, O God, in keeping with your mercy. In keeping with your unlimited compassion, wipe out my rebellious acts." 
Psalms51:2 Wash me thoroughly from my guilt, and cleanse me from my sin." 
Psalms51:3 I admit that I am rebellious. My sin is always in front of me. 
Psalms51:4 I have sinned against you, especially you. I have done what you consider evil. So you hand down justice when you speak, and you are blameless when you judge." 
Psalms51:5 Indeed, I was born guilty. I was a sinner when my mother conceived me." 
Psalms51:6 Yet, you desire truth and sincerity. Deep down inside me you teach me wisdom." 
Psalms51:7 Purify me from sin with hyssop, and I will be clean. Wash me, and I will be whiter than snow." 
Psalms51:8 Let me hear {sounds of} joy and gladness. Let the bones that you have broken dance. 
Psalms51:9 Hide your face from my sins, and wipe out all that I have done wrong." 
Psalms51:10 Create a clean heart in me, O God, and renew a faithful spirit within me." 
Psalms51:11 Do not force me away from your presence, and do not take your Holy Spirit from me." 
Psalms51:12 Restore the joy of your salvation to me, and provide me with a spirit of willing obedience." 
Psalms51:13 {Then} I will teach your ways to those who are rebellious, and sinners will return to you." 
Psalms51:14 Rescue me from the guilt of murder, O God, my savior. Let my tongue sing joyfully about your righteousness!" 
Psalms51:15 O Lord, open my lips, and my mouth will tell about your praise." 
Psalms51:16 You are not happy with any sacrifice. Otherwise, I would offer one {to you}. You are not pleased with burnt offerings." 
Psalms51:17 The sacrifice pleasing to God is a broken spirit. O God, you do not despise a broken and sorrowful heart." 
Psalms51:18 Favor Zion with your goodness. Rebuild the walls of Jerusalem. 
Psalms51:19 Then you will be pleased with sacrifices offered in the right spirit— with burnt offerings and whole burnt offerings. Young bulls will be offered on your altar. 
Psalms52:1 For the choir director; a maskil; a psalm by David when Doeg (who was from Edom) told Saul that David had come to Ahimelech's home. Why do you brag about the evil you've done, you hero? The mercy of God lasts all day long!" 
Psalms52:2 Your tongue makes up threats. It's like a sharp razor, you master of deceit." 
Psalms52:3 You prefer evil to good. You prefer lying to speaking the truth. Selah 
Psalms52:4 You love every destructive accusation, you deceitful tongue!" 
Psalms52:5 But God will ruin you forever. He will grab you and drag you out of your tent. He will pull your roots out of this world of the living. Selah 
Psalms52:6 Righteous people will see {this} and be struck with fear. They will laugh at you and say," 
Psalms52:7 Look at this person who refused to make God his fortress! Instead, he trusted his great wealth and became strong through his greed.""" 
Psalms52:8 But I am like a large olive tree in God's house. I trust the mercy of God forever and ever. 
Psalms52:9 I will give thanks to you forever for what you have done. In the presence of your godly people, I will wait with hope in your good name." 
Psalms53:1 For the choir director; according to mahalath, a maskil by David. Godless fools say in their hearts, ""There is no God."" They are corrupt. They do disgusting things. There is no one who does good things." 
Psalms53:2 God looks down from heaven on Adam's descendants to see if there is anyone who acts wisely, if there is anyone who seeks help from God." 
Psalms53:3 Everyone has fallen away. Together they have become rotten to the core. No one, not even one person, does good things." 
Psalms53:4 Are all those troublemakers, those who devour my people as if they were devouring food, so ignorant that they do not call on God?" 
Psalms53:5 There they are—panic-stricken— {but} there was no reason to panic, because God has scattered the bones of those who set up camp against you. You put them to shame. After all, God has rejected them." 
Psalms53:6 If only salvation for Israel would come from Zion! When God restores the fortunes of his people, Jacob will rejoice. Israel will be glad."
