Psalms54:2 O God, hear my prayer, and open your ears to the words from my mouth." 
Psalms54:3 Strangers have attacked me. Ruthless people seek my life. They do not think about God. Selah 
Psalms54:3 Strangers have attacked me. Ruthless people seek my life. They do not think about God. Selah 
Psalms54:4 God is my helper! The Lord is the provider for my life. 
Psalms54:5 My enemies spy on me. Pay them back with evil. Destroy them with your truth! 
Psalms54:6 I will make a sacrifice to you along with a freewill offering. I will give thanks to your good name, O Lord." 
Psalms54:7 Your name rescues me from every trouble. My eyes will gloat over my enemies. 
Psalms55:1 For the choir director; on stringed instruments; a maskil by David. Open your ears to my prayer, O God. Do not hide from my plea for mercy." 
Psalms55:2 Pay attention to me, and answer me. My thoughts are restless, and I am confused" 
Psalms55:3 because my enemy shouts at me and a wicked person persecutes me. They bring misery crashing down on me, and they attack me out of anger." 
Psalms55:4 My heart is in turmoil. The terrors of death have seized me. 
Psalms55:5 Fear and trembling have overcome me. Horror has overwhelmed me. 
Psalms55:6 I said, ""If only I had wings like a dove— I would fly away and find rest." 
Psalms55:7 Indeed, I would run far away. I would stay in the desert. Selah" 
Psalms55:8 I would hurry to find shelter from the raging wind and storm. 
Psalms55:9 Completely confuse their language, O Lord, because I see violence and conflict in the city." 
Psalms55:10 Day and night they go around on {top of} the city walls. Trouble and misery are everywhere. 
Psalms55:11 Destruction is everywhere. Oppression and fraud never leave the streets. 
Psalms55:12 If an enemy had insulted me, then I could bear it. If someone who hated me had attacked me, then I could hide from him." 
Psalms55:13 But it is you, my equal, my best friend, one I knew so well!" 
Psalms55:14 We used to talk to each other in complete confidence and walk into God's house with the festival crowds. 
Psalms55:15 Let death suddenly take {wicked people}! Let them go into the grave while they are still alive, because evil lives in their homes as well as in their hearts." 
Psalms55:16 But I call on God, and the Lord saves me." 
Psalms55:17 Morning, noon, and night I complain and groan, and he listens to my voice." 
Psalms55:18 With {his} peace, he will rescue my soul from the war waged against me, because there are many {soldiers fighting} against me." 
Psalms55:19 God will listen. The one who has sat enthroned from the beginning will deal with them. Selah They never change. They never fear God. 
Psalms55:20 {My best friend} has betrayed his friends. He has broken his solemn promise. 
Psalms55:21 His speech is smoother than butter, but there is war in his heart. His words are more soothing than oil, but they are like swords ready to attack." 
Psalms55:22 Turn your burdens over to the Lord, and he will take care of you. He will never let the righteous person stumble." 
Psalms55:23 But you, O God, will throw {wicked people} into the deepest pit. Bloodthirsty and deceitful people will not live out half their days. But I will trust you."

Psalms56:2 All day long my enemies spy on me. They harass me. There are so many fighting against me. 
Psalms56:3 Even when I am afraid, I still trust you." 
Psalms56:4 I praise the word of God. I trust God. I am not afraid. What can mere flesh {and blood} do to me? 
Psalms56:5 All day long my enemies twist my words. Their every thought is an evil plan against me. 
Psalms56:6 They attack, and then they hide. They watch my every step as they wait to take my life."

Psalms56:9 Then my enemies will retreat when I call {to you}. This I know: God is on my side.

Psalms56:11 I trust God. I am not afraid. What can mortals do to me?

Psalms57:1 For the choir director; al tashcheth; a miktam by David when he fled from Saul into the cave. Have pity on me, O God. Have pity on me, because my soul takes refuge in you. I will take refuge in the shadow of your wings until destructive storms pass by."

Psalms57:3 He sends his help from heaven and saves me. He disgraces the one who is harassing me. Selah God sends his mercy and his truth! 
Psalms57:4 My soul is surrounded by lions. I must lie down with man-eating lions. Their teeth are spears and arrows. Their tongues are sharp swords. 
Psalms57:5 May you be honored above the heavens, O God. Let your glory extend over the whole earth." 
Psalms57:6 {My enemies} spread out a net to catch me. (My soul is bowed down.) They dug a pit to trap me, but then they fell into it. Selah" 
Psalms57:7 My heart is confident, O God. My heart is confident. I want to sing and make music."

Psalms58:1 For the choir director; al tashcheth; a miktam by David. Do you rulers really give fair verdicts? Do you judge Adam's descendants fairly? 
Psalms58:2 No, you invent new crimes on earth, and your hands spread violence." 
Psalms58:3 {Even} inside the womb wicked people are strangers {to God}. From their birth liars go astray. 
Psalms58:4 They have poisonous venom like snakes. They are like a deaf cobra that shuts its ears 
Psalms58:5 so that it cannot hear the voice of a snake charmer or of anyone trained to cast spells. 
Psalms58:6 O God, knock the teeth out of their mouths. Break the young lions' teeth, O Lord." 
Psalms58:7 Let them disappear like water that drains away. When they aim their bows, let their arrows miss the target." 
Psalms58:8 Let them become like a snail that leaves behind a slimy trail or like a stillborn child who never sees the sun. 
Psalms58:9 Let {God} sweep them away faster than a cooking pot is heated by burning twigs. 
Psalms58:10 Righteous people will rejoice when they see {God} take revenge. They will wash their feet in the blood of wicked people. 
Psalms58:11 Then people will say, ""Righteous people certainly have a reward. There is a God who judges on earth."""

Psalms59:3 They lie in ambush for me right here! Fierce men attack me, O Lord, but not because of any disobedience," 
Psalms59:4 or any sin, or any guilt on my part. They hurry to take positions against me. Wake up, and help me; see {for yourself}." 
Psalms59:5 O Lord God of Armies, God of Israel, arise to punish all the nations. Have no pity on any traitors. Selah" 
Psalms59:6 They return in the evening. They howl like dogs. They prowl the city. 
Psalms59:7 See what pours out of their mouths— swords from their lips! {They think,} ""Who will hear us?""" 
Psalms59:8 O Lord, you laugh at them. You make fun of all the nations." 
Psalms59:9 O my strength, I watch for you! God is my stronghold, my merciful God!" 
Psalms59:10 God will come to meet me. He will let me gloat over those who spy on me. 
Psalms59:11 Do not kill them. Otherwise, my people may forget. Make them wander aimlessly by your power. Bring them down, O Lord, our shield," 
Psalms59:12 {because of} the sins from their mouths and the words on their lips. Let them be trapped by their own arrogance because they speak curses and lies. 
Psalms59:13 Destroy them in your rage. Destroy them until not one of them is left. Then they will know that God rules Jacob to the ends of the earth. Selah 
Psalms59:14 They return in the evening. They howl like dogs. They prowl the city. 
Psalms59:15 They wander around to find something to eat. If they are not full enough, they will stay all night." 
Psalms59:16 But I will sing about your strength. In the morning I will joyfully sing about your mercy. You have been my stronghold and a place of safety in times of trouble. 
Psalms59:17 O my strength, I will make music to praise you! God is my stronghold, my merciful God!" 
Psalms60:1 For the choir director; according to shushan eduth; a miktam by David; for teaching. When David fought Aram Naharaim and Aram Zobah, and {when} Joab came back and killed 12,000 men from Edom in the Dead Sea region. O God, you have rejected us. You have broken down our defenses. You have been angry. Restore us!" 
Psalms60:2 You made the land quake. You split it wide open. Heal the cracks in it because it is falling apart. 
Psalms60:3 You have made your people experience hardships. You have given us wine that makes us stagger. 
Psalms60:4 Yet, you have raised a flag for those who fear you so that they can rally to it when attacked by bows {and arrows}. Selah" 
Psalms60:5 Save {us} with your powerful hand, and answer us so that those who are dear to you may be rescued." 
Psalms60:6 God has promised the following through his holiness: I will triumph! I will divide Shechem. I will measure the valley of Succoth. 
Psalms60:7 Gilead is mine. Manasseh is mine. Ephraim is the helmet on my head. Judah is my scepter. 
Psalms60:8 Moab is my washtub. I will throw my shoe over Edom. I will shout in triumph over Philistia. 
Psalms60:9 Who will bring me into the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom? 
Psalms60:10 Isn't it you, O God, who rejected us? Isn't it you, O God, who refused to accompany our armies?" 
Psalms60:11 Give us help against the enemy because human assistance is worthless. 
Psalms60:12 With God we will display great strength. He will trample our enemies.

Psalms62:1 For the choir director; according to Jeduthun; a psalm by David. My soul waits calmly for God alone. My salvation comes from him. 
Psalms62:2 He alone is my rock and my savior—my stronghold. I cannot be severely shaken. 
Psalms62:3 How long will all of you attack a person? How long will you try to murder him, as though he were a leaning wall or a sagging fence?" 
Psalms62:4 They plan to force him out of his high position. They are happy to lie. They bless with their mouths, but in their hearts they curse. Selah" 
Psalms62:5 Wait calmly for God alone, my soul, because my hope comes from him." 
Psalms62:6 He alone is my rock and my savior—my stronghold. I cannot be shaken. 
Psalms62:7 My salvation and my glory depend on God. God is the rock of my strength, my refuge."

Psalms62:10 Do not count on extortion {to make you rich}. Do not hope to gain anything through robbery. When riches increase, do not depend on them."

Psalms62:12 Mercy belongs to you, O Lord. You reward a person based on what he has done.""" 
Psalms63:1 A psalm by David when he was in the wilderness of Judah. O God, you are my God. At dawn I search for you. My soul thirsts for you. My body longs for you in a dry, parched land where there is no water."

Psalms63:3 My lips will praise you because your mercy is better than life {itself}. 
Psalms63:4 So I will thank you as long as I live. I will lift up my hands {to pray} in your name. 
Psalms63:5 You satisfy my soul with the richest foods. My mouth will sing {your} praise with joyful lips. 
Psalms63:6 As I lie on my bed, I remember you. Through the long hours of the night, I think about you."

Psalms64:7 But God will shoot them with an arrow. Suddenly, they will be struck dead."

Psalms65:2 You are the one who hears prayers. Everyone will come to you. 
Psalms65:3 Various sins overwhelm me. You are the one who forgives our rebellious acts.

Psalms65:5 You answer us with awe-inspiring acts {done} in righteousness, O God, our savior, the hope of all the ends of the earth and of the most distant sea," 
Psalms65:6 the one who set the mountains in place with his strength, the one who is clothed with power," 
Psalms65:7 the one who calms the roar of the seas, their crashing waves, and the uproar of the nations." 
Psalms65:8 Those who live at the ends of the earth are in awe of your miraculous signs. The lands of the morning sunrise and evening sunset sing joyfully. 
Psalms65:9 You take care of the earth, and you water it. You make it much richer than it was. (The river of God is filled with water.) You provide grain for them. Indeed, you even prepare the ground." 
Psalms65:10 You drench plowed fields {with rain} and level their clumps of soil. You soften them with showers and bless what grows in them.

Psalms65:13 The pastures are covered with flocks. The valleys are carpeted with grain. All of them shout triumphantly. Indeed, they sing."

Psalms68:1 For the choir director; a psalm by David; a song. God will arise. His enemies will be scattered. Those who hate him will flee from him. 
Psalms68:2 Blow them away like smoke. Let wicked people melt in God's presence like wax next to a fire. 
Psalms68:3 But let righteous people rejoice. Let them celebrate in God's presence. Let them overflow with joy. 
Psalms68:4 Sing to God; make music to praise his name. Make a highway for him to ride through the deserts. The Lord is his name. Celebrate in his presence. 
Psalms68:5 The God who is in his holy dwelling place is the father of the fatherless and the defender of widows. 
Psalms68:6 God places lonely people in families. He leads prisoners out of prison into productive lives, but rebellious people must live in an unproductive land." 
Psalms68:7 O God, when you went in front of your people, when you marched through the desert, Selah" 
Psalms68:8 the earth quaked and the sky poured in the presence of the God of Sinai, in the presence of the God of Israel." 
Psalms68:9 You watered the land with plenty of rain, O God. You refreshed it when your land was exhausted." 
Psalms68:10 Your flock settled there. Out of your goodness, O God, you provided for oppressed people." 
Psalms68:11 The Lord gives instructions. The women who announce the good news are a large army. 
Psalms68:12 {They say,} ""The kings of the armies flee; they run away. The women who remained at home will divide the goods." 
Psalms68:13 Though you stayed among the sheep pens, {you will be like} the wings of a dove covered with silver, its feathers with yellow gold." 
Psalms68:14 Meanwhile, the Almighty was still scattering kings there like snow falling on Mount Zalmon.""" 
Psalms68:15 The mountain of Bashan is the mountain of God. The mountain of Bashan is the mountain with many peaks. 
Psalms68:16 Why do you look with envy, you mountains with many peaks, at the mountain where God has chosen to live? Certainly, the Lord will live there forever." 
Psalms68:17 The chariots of God are twenty thousand in number, thousands upon thousands. The Lord is among them. {The God of} Sinai is in his holy place." 
Psalms68:18 You went to the highest place. You took prisoners captive. You received gifts from people, even from rebellious people, so that the Lord God may live there." 
Psalms68:19 Thanks be to the Lord, who daily carries our burdens for us. God is our salvation. Selah" 
Psalms68:20 Our God is the God of victories. The Almighty Lord is our escape from death. 
Psalms68:21 Certainly, God will crush the heads of his enemies {and destroy even} the hair on the heads of those who continue to be guilty." 
Psalms68:22 The Lord said, ""I will bring them back from Bashan. I will bring them back from the depths of the sea" 
Psalms68:23 so that you, {my people,} may bathe your feet in blood and the tongues of your dogs may lick the blood of your enemies.""" 
Psalms68:24 Your festival processions, O God, can be seen by everyone. They are the processions for my God, my king, into the holy place." 
Psalms68:25 The singers are in front. The musicians are behind them. The young women beating tambourines are between them. 
Psalms68:26 Thank God, the Lord, the source of Israel, with the choirs." 
Psalms68:27 Benjamin, the youngest, is leading them, {next} the leaders of Judah with their noisy crowds, {then} the leaders of Zebulun, {then} the leaders of Naphtali." 
Psalms68:28 Your God has decided you will be strong. Display your strength, O God, as you have for us before." 
Psalms68:29 Kings will bring you gifts because of your temple high above Jerusalem. 
Psalms68:30 Threaten the beast who is among the cattails, the herd of bulls with the calves of the nations, until it humbles itself with pieces of silver. Scatter the people who find joy in war." 
Psalms68:31 Ambassadors will come from Egypt. Sudan will stretch out its hands to God {in prayer}. 
Psalms68:32 You kingdoms of the world, sing to God. Make music to praise the Lord. Selah" 
Psalms68:33 God rides through the ancient heaven, the highest heaven. Listen! He makes his voice heard, his powerful voice." 
Psalms68:34 Acknowledge the power of God. His majesty is over Israel, and his power is in the skies." 
Psalms68:35 God, the God of Israel, is awe-inspiring in his holy place. He gives strength and power to his people. Thanks be to God!" 
Psalms69:1 For the choir director; according to shoshannim; by David. Save me, O God! The water is already up to my neck!" 
Psalms69:2 I am sinking in deep mud. There is nothing to stand on. I am in deep water. A flood is sweeping me away. 
Psalms69:3 I am exhausted from crying for help. My throat is hoarse. My eyes are strained {from} looking for my God. 
Psalms69:4 Those who hate me for no reason outnumber the hairs on my head. Those who want to destroy me are mighty. They have no reason to be my enemies. I am forced to pay back what I did not steal. 
Psalms69:5 O God, you know my stupidity, and the things of which I am guilty are not hidden from you." 
Psalms69:6 Do not let those who wait with hope for you be put to shame because of me, O Almighty Lord of Armies. Do not let those who come to you for help be humiliated because of me, O God of Israel." 
Psalms69:7 Indeed, for your sake I have endured insults. Humiliation has covered my face." 
Psalms69:8 I have become a stranger to my {own} brothers, a foreigner to my mother's sons." 
Psalms69:9 Indeed, devotion for your house has consumed me, and the insults of those who insult you have fallen on me." 
Psalms69:10 I cried and fasted, but I was insulted for it." 
Psalms69:11 I dressed myself in sackcloth, but I became the object of ridicule." 
Psalms69:12 Those who sit at the gate gossip about me, and drunkards make up songs about me." 
Psalms69:13 May my prayer come to you at an acceptable time, O Lord. O God, out of the greatness of your mercy, answer me with the truth of your salvation." 
Psalms69:14 Rescue me from the mud. Do not let me sink {into it}. I want to be rescued from those who hate me and from the deep water. 
Psalms69:15 Do not let floodwaters sweep me away. Do not let the ocean swallow me up, or the pit close its mouth over me." 
Psalms69:16 Answer me, O Lord, because your mercy is good. Out of your unlimited compassion, turn to me." 
Psalms69:17 I am in trouble, so do not hide your face from me. Answer me quickly!" 
Psalms69:18 Come close, and defend my soul. Set me free because of my enemies." 
Psalms69:19 You know that I have been insulted, put to shame, and humiliated. All my opponents are in front of you." 
Psalms69:20 Insults have broken my heart, and I am sick. I looked for sympathy, but there was none. I looked for people to comfort me, but I found no one." 
Psalms69:21 They poisoned my food, and when I was thirsty, they gave me vinegar to drink." 
Psalms69:22 Let the table set for them become a trap and a snare for their friends. 
Psalms69:23 Let their vision become clouded so that they cannot see. Let their thighs continually shake. 
Psalms69:24 Pour your rage on them. Let your burning anger catch up with them. 
Psalms69:25 Let their camp be deserted and their tents empty. 
Psalms69:26 They persecute the one you have struck, and they talk about the pain of those you have wounded." 
Psalms69:27 Charge them with one crime after another. Do not let them be found innocent. 
Psalms69:28 Let their {names} be erased from the Book of Life. Do not let them be listed with righteous people. 
Psalms69:29 I am suffering and in pain. Let your saving power protect me, O God." 
Psalms69:30 I want to praise the name of God with a song. I want to praise its greatness with a song of thanksgiving. 
Psalms69:31 This will please the Lord more than {sacrificing} an ox or a bull with horns and hoofs. 
Psalms69:32 Oppressed people will see {this} and rejoice. May the hearts of those who look to God for help be refreshed. 
Psalms69:33 The Lord listens to needy people. He does not despise his own who are in prison. 
Psalms69:34 Let heaven and earth, the seas, and everything that moves in them, praise him." 
Psalms69:35 When God saves Zion, he will rebuild the cities of Judah. His servants will live there and take possession of it." 
Psalms69:36 The descendants of his servants will inherit it. Those who love him will live there. 
Psalms70:1 For the choir director; by David; to be kept in mind. Come quickly to rescue me, O God! Come quickly to help me, O Lord!" 
Psalms70:2 Let those who seek my life be confused and put to shame. Let those who want my downfall be turned back and disgraced. 
Psalms70:3 Let those who say, ""Aha! Aha!"" be turned back because of their own shame." 
Psalms70:4 Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad because of you. Let those who love your salvation continually say, ""God is great!""" 
Psalms70:5 But I am oppressed and needy. O God, come to me quickly. You are my help and my savior. O Lord, do not delay!" 
Psalms71:1 I have taken refuge in you, O Lord. Never let me be put to shame." 
Psalms71:2 Rescue me and free me because of your righteousness. Turn your ear toward me, and save me." 
Psalms71:3 Be a rock on which I may live, a place where I may always go. You gave the order to save me! Indeed, you are my rock and my fortress." 
Psalms71:4 My God, free me from the hands of a wicked person, from the grasp of one who is cruel and unjust." 
Psalms71:5 You are my hope, O Almighty Lord. You have been my confidence ever since I was young." 
Psalms71:6 I depended on you before I was born. You took me from my mother's womb. My songs of praise constantly speak about you. 
Psalms71:7 I have become an example to many people, but you are my strong refuge." 
Psalms71:8 My mouth is filled with your praise, with your glory all day long." 
Psalms71:9 Do not reject me when I am old or abandon me when I lose my strength. 
Psalms71:10 My enemies talk about me. They watch me as they plot to take my life. 
Psalms71:11 They say, ""God has abandoned him. Pursue him and grab him because there is no one to rescue him.""" 
Psalms71:12 O God, do not be so distant from me. O my God, come quickly to help me." 
Psalms71:13 Let those who accuse me come to a shameful end. Let those who want my downfall be covered with disgrace and humiliation. 
Psalms71:14 But I will always have hope. I will praise you more and more. 
Psalms71:15 My mouth will tell about your righteousness, about your salvation all day long. Even then, it is more than I can understand." 
Psalms71:16 I will come with the mighty deeds of the Almighty Lord. I will praise your righteousness, yours alone." 
Psalms71:17 O God, you have taught me ever since I was young, and I still talk about the miracles you have done." 
Psalms71:18 Even when I am old and gray, do not abandon me, O God. Let me live to tell the people of this age what your strength has accomplished, to tell about your power to all who will come." 
Psalms71:19 Your righteousness reaches to the heavens, O God. You have done great things. O God, who is like you?" 
Psalms71:20 You have made me endure many terrible troubles. You restore me to life again. You bring me back from the depths of the earth. 
Psalms71:21 You comfort me and make me greater than ever. 
Psalms71:22 Because of your faithfulness, O my God, even I will give thanks to you as I play on a lyre. I will make music with a harp to praise you, O Holy One of Israel." 
Psalms71:23 My lips will sing with joy when I make music to praise you. My soul, which you have rescued, also will sing joyfully." 
Psalms71:24 My tongue will tell about your righteousness all day long, because those who wanted my downfall have been disgraced and put to shame." 
Psalms72:1 By Solomon. O God, give the king your justice and the king's son your righteousness" 
Psalms72:2 so that he may judge your people with righteousness and your oppressed {people} with justice. 
Psalms72:3 May the mountains bring peace to the people and the hills bring righteousness. 
Psalms72:4 May he grant justice to the people who are oppressed. May he save the children of needy people and crush their oppressor. 
Psalms72:5 May they fear you as long as the sun and moon {shine}— throughout every generation. 
Psalms72:6 May he be like rain that falls on {freshly} cut grass, like showers that water the land." 
Psalms72:7 May righteous people blossom in his day. May there be unlimited peace until the moon no longer {shines}. 
Psalms72:8 May he rule from sea to sea, from the Euphrates River to the ends of the earth." 
Psalms72:9 May the people of the desert kneel in front of him. May his enemies lick the dust. 
Psalms72:10 May the kings from Tarshish and the islands bring presents. May the kings from Sheba and Seba bring gifts. 
Psalms72:11 May all kings worship him. May all nations serve him. 
Psalms72:12 He will rescue the needy person who cries for help and the oppressed person who has no one's help. 
Psalms72:13 He will have pity on the poor and needy and will save the lives of the needy. 
Psalms72:14 He will rescue them from oppression and violence. Their blood will be precious in his sight. 
Psalms72:15 May he live long. May the gold from Sheba be given to him. May {the people} pray for him continually. May {they} praise him all day long. 
Psalms72:16 May there be plenty of grain in the land. May it wave {in the breeze} on the mountaintops, its fruit like {the treetops of} Lebanon. May those from the city flourish like the grass on the ground." 
Psalms72:17 May his name endure forever. May his name continue as long as the sun {shines}. May all nations be blessed through him and call him blessed. 
Psalms72:18 Thank the Lord God, the God of Israel, who alone does miracles." 
Psalms72:19 Thanks be to his glorious name forever. May the whole earth be filled with his glory. Amen and amen! 
Psalms72:20 The prayers by David, son of Jesse, end here." 
Psalms73:1 A psalm by Asaph. God is truly good to Israel, to those whose lives are pure." 
Psalms73:2 But my feet had almost stumbled. They had almost slipped 
Psalms73:3 because I was envious of arrogant people when I saw the prosperity that wicked people enjoy. 
Psalms73:4 They suffer no pain. Their bodies are healthy. 
Psalms73:5 They have no drudgery in their lives like ordinary people. They are not plagued {with problems} like others. 
Psalms73:6 That is why they wear arrogance like a necklace and acts of violence like clothing. 
Psalms73:7 Their eyes peer out from their fat faces, and their imaginations run wild." 
Psalms73:8 They ridicule. They speak maliciously. They speak arrogantly about oppression. 
Psalms73:9 They verbally attack heaven, and they order people around on earth." 
Psalms73:10 That is why God's people turn to wickedness and swallow their words. 
Psalms73:11 Then wicked people ask, ""What does God know?"" ""Does the Most High know anything?""" 
Psalms73:12 Look how wicked they are! They never have a worry. They grow more and more wealthy. 
Psalms73:13 I've received no reward for keeping my life pure and washing my hands of any blame. 
Psalms73:14 I'm plagued {with problems} all day long, and every morning my punishment {begins again}." 
Psalms73:15 If I had said, ""I will continue to talk like that,"" I would have betrayed God's people." 
Psalms73:16 But when I tried to understand this, it was too difficult for me." 
Psalms73:17 Only when I came into God's holy place did I {finally} understand what would happen to them. 
Psalms73:18 You put them in slippery places and make them fall into ruin. 
Psalms73:19 They are suddenly destroyed. They are completely swept away by terror! 
Psalms73:20 As {someone} gets rid of a dream when he wakes up, so you, O Lord, get rid of the thought of them when you wake up." 
Psalms73:21 When my heart was filled with bitterness and my mind was seized {with envy}," 
Psalms73:22 I was stupid, and I did not understand. I was like a dumb animal in your presence." 
Psalms73:23 Yet, I am always with you. You hold on to my right hand." 
Psalms73:24 With your advice you guide me, and in the end you will take me to glory." 
Psalms73:25 As long as I have you, I don't need anyone else in heaven or on earth." 
Psalms73:26 My body and mind may waste away, but God remains the foundation of my life and my inheritance forever." 
Psalms73:27 Without a doubt, those who are far from you will die. You destroy all who are unfaithful to you." 
Psalms73:28 Being united with God is my highest good. I have made the Almighty Lord my refuge so that I may report everything that he has done. 
Psalms74:1 A maskil by Asaph. Why, O God, have you rejected us forever? Why does your anger smolder against the sheep in your care?" 
Psalms74:2 Remember your congregation. Long ago you made it your own. You bought this tribe to be your possession. This tribe is Mount Zion, where you have made your home." 
Psalms74:3 Turn your steps toward these pathetic ruins. The enemy has destroyed everything in the holy temple. 
Psalms74:4 Your opponents have roared inside your meeting place. They have set up their own emblems as symbols. 
Psalms74:5 Starting from its entrance, they hacked away like a woodcutter in a forest." 
Psalms74:6 They smashed all its carved paneling with axes and hatchets. 
Psalms74:7 They burned your holy place to the ground. They dishonored the place where you live among us. 
Psalms74:8 They said to themselves, ""We will crush them."" They burned every meeting place of God in the land." 
Psalms74:9 We no longer see miraculous signs. There are no prophets anymore. No one knows how long this will last. 
Psalms74:10 How long, O God, will the enemy insult us? Will the enemy despise you forever?" 
Psalms74:11 Why do you hold back your hand, especially your right hand? Take your hands out of your pockets. Destroy your enemies!" 
Psalms74:12 And yet, from long ago God has been my king, the one who has been victorious throughout the earth." 
Psalms74:13 You stirred up the sea with your own strength. You smashed the heads of sea monsters in the water. 
Psalms74:14 You crushed the heads of Leviathan and gave them to the creatures of the desert for food. 
Psalms74:15 You opened the springs and brooks. You dried up the ever-flowing rivers. 
Psalms74:16 The day and the night are yours. You set the moon and the sun in their places. 
Psalms74:17 You determined all the boundaries of the earth. You created summer and winter. 
Psalms74:18 Remember how the enemy insulted you, O Lord. Remember how an entire nation of godless fools despised your name." 
Psalms74:19 Do not hand over the soul of your dove to wild animals. Do not forget the life of your oppressed people forever. 
Psalms74:20 Consider your promise because every dark corner of the land is filled with violence. 
Psalms74:21 Do not let oppressed people come back in disgrace. Let weak and needy people praise your name. 
Psalms74:22 Arise, O God! Fight for your own cause! Remember how godless fools insult you all day long." 
Psalms74:23 Do not forget the shouting of your opponents. Do not forget the uproar made by those who attack you. 
Psalms75:1 For the choir director; al tashcheth; a psalm by Asaph; a song. We give thanks to you, O God; we give thanks. You are present, and your miracles confirm that." 
Psalms75:2 When I choose the right time, I will judge fairly." 
Psalms75:3 When the earth and everyone who lives on it begin to melt, I will make its foundations as solid as rock. Selah" 
Psalms75:4 I said to those who brag, ""Don't brag,"" and to wicked people, ""Don't raise your weapons." 
Psalms75:5 Don't raise your weapons so proudly or speak so defiantly. 
Psalms75:6 The {authority} to reward someone does not {come} from the east, from the west, or {even} from the wilderness." 
Psalms75:7 God alone is the judge. He punishes one person and rewards another. 
Psalms75:8 A cup is in the Lord's hand. (Its foaming wine is thoroughly mixed with spices.) He will empty it, {and} all the wicked people on earth will have to drink every last drop." 
Psalms75:9 But I will speak {about your miracles} forever. I will make music to praise the God of Jacob. 
Psalms75:10 I will destroy all the weapons of wicked people, but the weapons of righteous people will be raised proudly." 
Psalms76:1 For the choir director; on stringed instruments; a psalm by Asaph; a song. God is known in Judah. His name is great in Israel. 
Psalms76:2 His tent is in Salem. His home is in Zion. 
Psalms76:3 There he destroyed flaming arrows, shields, swords, and weapons of war. Selah" 
Psalms76:4 You are the radiant one. You are more majestic than the ancient mountains. 
Psalms76:5 Brave people were robbed. They died. None of the warriors were able to lift a hand. 
Psalms76:6 At your stern warning, O God of Jacob, chariot riders and horses were put to sleep." 
Psalms76:7 You alone must be feared! Who can stand in your presence when you become angry? 
Psalms76:8 From heaven you announced a verdict. The earth was fearful and silent 
Psalms76:9 when you rose to judge, O God, when you rose to save every oppressed person on earth. Selah" 
Psalms76:10 Even angry mortals will praise you. You will wear the remainder of {their} anger. 
Psalms76:11 Make vows to the Lord your God, and keep them. Let everyone around him bring gifts to the one who must be feared." 
Psalms76:12 He cuts short the lives of influential people. He terrifies the kings of the earth. 
Psalms77:1 For the choir director; according to Jeduthun; a psalm by Asaph. Loudly, I cried to God. Loudly, I cried to God so that he would open his ears to {hear} me." 
Psalms77:2 On the day I was in trouble, I went to the Lord for help. At night I stretched out my hands in prayer without growing tired. Yet, my soul refused to be comforted." 
Psalms77:3 I sigh as I remember God. I begin to lose hope as I think about him. Selah 
Psalms77:4 (You keep my eyelids open.) I am so upset that I cannot speak. 
Psalms77:5 I have considered the days of old, the years long ago." 
Psalms77:6 I remember my song in the night and reflect {on it}. My spirit searches {for an answer}: 
Psalms77:7 Will the Lord reject {me} for all time? Will he ever accept me? 
Psalms77:8 Has his mercy come to an end forever? Has his promise been canceled throughout every generation? 
Psalms77:9 Has God forgotten to be merciful? Has he locked up his compassion because of his anger? Selah 
Psalms77:10 Then I said, ""It makes me feel sick that the power of the Most High is no longer the same.""" 
Psalms77:11 I will remember the deeds of the Lord. I will remember your ancient miracles. 
Psalms77:12 I will reflect on all your actions and think about what you have done. 
Psalms77:13 O God, your ways are holy! What god is as great as our God?" 
Psalms77:14 You are the God who performs miracles. You have made your strength known among the nations. 
Psalms77:15 With your might you have defended your people, the descendants of Jacob and Joseph. Selah" 
Psalms77:16 The water saw you, O God. The water saw you and shook. Even the depths of the sea trembled." 
Psalms77:17 The clouds poured out water. The sky thundered. Even your arrows flashed in every direction. 
Psalms77:18 The sound of your thunder rumbled in the sky. Streaks of lightning lit up the world. The earth trembled and shook. 
Psalms77:19 Your road went through the sea. Your path went through raging water, but your footprints could not be seen." 
Psalms77:20 Like a shepherd, you led your people. You had Moses and Aaron take them by the hand." 
Psalms78:1 A maskil by Asaph. Open your ears to my teachings, my people. Turn your ears to the words from my mouth." 
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth to illustrate points. I will explain what has been hidden long ago," 
Psalms78:3 things that we have heard and known about, things that our parents have told us." 
Psalms78:4 We will not hide them from our children. We will tell the next generation about the Lord's power and great deeds and the miraculous things he has done. 
Psalms78:5 He established written instructions for Jacob's people. He gave his teachings to Israel. He commanded our ancestors to make them known to their children 
Psalms78:6 so that the next generation would know them. Children yet to be born {would learn them}. They will grow up and tell their children 
Psalms78:7 to trust God, to remember what he has done, and to obey his commands." 
Psalms78:8 Then they will not be like their ancestors, a stubborn and rebellious generation. Their hearts were not loyal. Their spirits were not faithful to God." 
Psalms78:9 The men of Ephraim, well-equipped with bows {and arrows}, turned {and ran} on the day of battle." 
Psalms78:10 They had not been faithful to God's promise. They refused to follow his teachings. 
Psalms78:11 They forgot what he had done— the miracles that he had shown them. 
Psalms78:12 In front of their ancestors he performed miracles in the land of Egypt, in the fields of Zoan." 
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea and led them through it. He made the waters stand up like a wall. 
Psalms78:14 He guided them by a cloud during the day and by a fiery light throughout the night. 
Psalms78:15 He split rocks in the desert. He gave them plenty to drink, an ocean of water." 
Psalms78:16 He made streams come out of a rock. He made the water flow like rivers. 
Psalms78:17 They continued to sin against him, to rebel in the desert against the Most High." 
Psalms78:18 They deliberately tested God by demanding the food they craved. 
Psalms78:19 They spoke against God by saying, ""Can God prepare a banquet in the desert?" 
Psalms78:20 True, he did strike a rock, and water did gush out, and the streams did overflow. But can he also give us bread or provide us, his people, with meat?""" 
Psalms78:21 When the Lord heard this, he became furious. His fire burned against Jacob and his anger flared up at Israel" 
Psalms78:22 because they did not believe God or trust him to save them. 
Psalms78:23 In spite of that, he commanded the clouds above and opened the doors of heaven." 
Psalms78:24 He rained manna down on them to eat and gave them grain from heaven. 
Psalms78:25 Humans ate the bread of the mighty ones, and God sent them plenty of food." 
Psalms78:26 He made the east wind blow in the heavens and guided the south wind with his might. 
Psalms78:27 He rained meat down on them like dust, birds like the sand on the seashore." 
Psalms78:28 He made the birds fall in the middle of his camp, all around his dwelling place." 
Psalms78:29 They ate more than enough. He gave them what they wanted," 
Psalms78:30 but they still wanted more. While the food was still in their mouths," 
Psalms78:31 the anger of God flared up against them. He killed their strongest men and slaughtered the best young men in Israel. 
Psalms78:32 In spite of all this, they continued to sin, and they no longer believed in his miracles." 
Psalms78:33 He brought their days to an end like a whisper in the wind. He brought their years to an end in terror. 
Psalms78:34 When he killed {some of} them, {the rest} searched for him. They turned from their sins and eagerly looked for God." 
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their rock, that the Most High was their defender." 
Psalms78:36 They flattered him with their mouths and lied to him with their tongues. 
Psalms78:37 Their hearts were not loyal to him. They were not faithful to his promise. 
Psalms78:38 But he is compassionate. He forgave their sin. He did not destroy them. He restrained his anger many times. He did not display all of his fury. 
Psalms78:39 He remembered that they were only flesh and blood, a breeze that blows and does not return." 
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness! How often they caused him grief in the desert! 
Psalms78:41 Again and again they tested God, and they pushed the Holy One of Israel to the limit." 
Psalms78:42 They did not remember his power— the day he freed them from their oppressor," 
Psalms78:43 when he performed his miraculous signs in Egypt, his wonders in the fields of Zoan." 
Psalms78:44 He turned their rivers into blood so that they could not drink from their streams. 
Psalms78:45 He sent a swarm of flies that bit them and frogs that ruined them. 
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to grasshoppers and their produce to locusts. 
Psalms78:47 He killed their vines with hail and their fig trees with frost. 
Psalms78:48 He let the hail strike their cattle and bolts of lightning strike their livestock. 
Psalms78:49 He sent his burning anger, rage, fury, and hostility against them. He sent an army of destroying angels." 
Psalms78:50 He cleared a path for his anger. He did not spare them. He let the plague take their lives. 
Psalms78:51 He slaughtered every firstborn in Egypt, the ones born in the tents of Ham when their fathers were young." 
Psalms78:52 But he led his own people out like sheep and guided them like a flock through the wilderness. 
Psalms78:53 He led them safely. They had no fear while the sea covered their enemies. 
Psalms78:54 He brought them into his holy land, to this mountain that his power had won." 
Psalms78:55 He forced nations out of their way and gave them the land of the nations as their inheritance. He settled the tribes of Israel in their own tents. 
Psalms78:56 They tested God Most High and rebelled against him. They did not obey his written instructions. 
Psalms78:57 They were disloyal and treacherous like their ancestors. They were like arrows shot from a defective bow. 
Psalms78:58 They made him angry because of their illegal worship sites. They made him furious because they worshiped idols. 
Psalms78:59 When God heard, he became furious. He completely rejected Israel." 
Psalms78:60 He abandoned his dwelling place in Shiloh, the tent where he had lived among humans." 
Psalms78:61 He allowed his power to be taken captive and handed his glory over to an oppressor. 
Psalms78:62 He let swords kill his people. He was furious with those who belonged to him. 
Psalms78:63 Fire consumed his best young men, so his virgins heard no wedding songs." 
Psalms78:64 His priests were cut down with swords. The widows {of his priests} could not even weep {for them}. 
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord woke up like one who had been sleeping, like a warrior sobering up from {too much} wine." 
Psalms78:66 He struck his enemies from behind and disgraced them forever. 
Psalms78:67 He rejected the tent of Joseph. He did not choose the tribe of Ephraim," 
Psalms78:68 but he chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which he loved." 
Psalms78:69 He built his holy place to be like the high heavens, like the earth which he made to last for a long time." 
Psalms78:70 He chose his servant David. He took him from the sheep pens. 
Psalms78:71 He brought him from tending the ewes that had lambs so that David could be the shepherd of the people of Jacob, of Israel, the people who belonged to the Lord." 
Psalms78:72 With unselfish devotion David became their shepherd. With skill he guided them. 
Psalms79:1 A psalm by Asaph. O God, the nations have invaded the land that belongs to you. They have dishonored your holy temple. They have left Jerusalem in ruins." 
Psalms79:2 They have given the dead bodies of your servants to the birds for food. They have given the flesh of your godly ones to the animals. 
Psalms79:3 They have shed the blood of your people around Jerusalem as though it were water. There is no one to bury your people. 
Psalms79:4 We have become a disgrace to our neighbors, an object of ridicule and contempt to those around us." 
Psalms79:5 How long, O Lord? Will you remain angry forever? Will your fury continue to burn like fire?" 
Psalms79:6 Pour your fury on the nations that do not know you, on the kingdoms that have not called you." 
Psalms79:7 They have devoured Jacob. They have destroyed his home. 
Psalms79:8 Do not hold the crimes of our ancestors against us. Reach out to us soon with your compassion, because we are helpless." 
Psalms79:9 Help us, O God, our savior, for the glory of your name. Rescue us, and forgive our sins for the honor of your name." 
Psalms79:10 Why should the nations {be allowed to} say, ""Where is their God?"" Let us watch as the nations learn that there is punishment for shedding the blood of your servants." 
Psalms79:11 Let the groans of prisoners come into your presence. With your powerful arm rescue those who are condemned to death. 
Psalms79:12 Pay each one of our neighbors back with seven times the number of insults they used to insult you, O Lord." 
Psalms79:13 Then we, your people, the flock in your pasture, will give thanks to you forever. We will praise you throughout every generation." 
Psalms80:1 For the choir director; according to shoshannim eduth; by Asaph; a psalm. Open your ears, O Shepherd of Israel, the one who leads {the descendants of} Joseph like sheep, the one who is enthroned over the angels." 
Psalms80:2 Appear in front of Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh. Wake up your power, and come to save us." 
Psalms80:3 O God, restore us and smile on us so that we may be saved." 
Psalms80:4 O Lord God, commander of armies, how long will you smolder in anger against the prayer of your people?" 
Psalms80:5 You made them eat tears as food. You often made them drink {their own} tears. 
Psalms80:6 You made us a source of conflict to our neighbors, and our enemies made fun of us." 
Psalms80:7 O God, commander of armies, restore us and smile on us so that we may be saved." 
Psalms80:8 You brought a vine from Egypt. You forced out the nations and planted it. 
Psalms80:9 You cleared the ground for it so that it took root and filled the land. 
Psalms80:10 Its shade covered the mountains. Its branches covered the mighty cedars. 
Psalms80:11 It reached out with its branches to the Mediterranean Sea. Its shoots reached the Euphrates River. 
Psalms80:12 Why did you break down the stone fences around this vine? All who pass by are picking its fruit. 
Psalms80:13 Wild boars from the forest graze on it. Wild animals devour it. 
Psalms80:14 O God, commander of armies, come back! Look from heaven and see! Come to help this vine." 
Psalms80:15 Take care of what your right hand planted, the son you strengthened for yourself." 
Psalms80:16 The vine has been cut down and burned. Let them be destroyed by the threatening look on your face. 
Psalms80:17 Let your power rest on the man you have chosen, the son of man you strengthened for yourself." 
Psalms80:18 Then we will never turn away from you. Give us life again, and we will call on you." 
Psalms80:19 O Lord God, commander of armies, restore us, and smile on us so that we may be saved." 
Psalms81:1 For the choir director; on the gittith; by Asaph. Sing joyfully to God, our strength. Shout happily to the God of Jacob." 
Psalms81:2 Begin a psalm, and strike a tambourine. Play lyres and harps with their pleasant music." 
Psalms81:3 Blow the ram's horn on the day of the new moon, on the day of the full moon, on our festival days." 
Psalms81:4 This is a law for Israel, a legal decision from the God of Jacob." 
Psalms81:5 These are the instructions God set in place for Joseph when Joseph rose to power over Egypt. I heard a message I did not understand: 
Psalms81:6 I removed the burden from his shoulder. His hands were freed from the basket. 
Psalms81:7 When you were in trouble, you called out {to me}, and I rescued you. I was hidden in thunder, but I answered you. I tested your {loyalty} at the oasis of Meribah. Selah" 
Psalms81:8 Listen, my people, and I will warn you. Israel, if you would only listen to me!" 
Psalms81:9 Never keep any strange god among you. Never worship a foreign god. 
Psalms81:10 I am the Lord your God, the one who brought you out of Egypt. Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it." 
Psalms81:11 But my people did not listen to me. Israel wanted nothing to do with me. 
Psalms81:12 So I let them go their own stubborn ways and follow their own advice. 
Psalms81:13 If only my people would listen to me! If only Israel would follow me! 
Psalms81:14 I would quickly defeat their enemies. I would turn my power against their foes. 
Psalms81:15 Those who hate the Lord would cringe in front of him, and their time {for punishment} would last forever." 
Psalms81:16 But I would feed Israel with the finest wheat and satisfy them with honey from a rock. 
Psalms82:1 A psalm by Asaph. God takes his place in his own assembly. He pronounces judgment among the gods: 
Psalms82:2 How long are you going to judge unfairly? How long are you going to side with wicked people? Selah 
Psalms82:3 Defend weak people and orphans. Protect the rights of the oppressed and the poor. 
Psalms82:4 Rescue weak and needy people. Help them escape the power of wicked people. 
Psalms82:5 Wicked people do not know or understand anything. As they walk around in the dark, all the foundations of the earth shake." 
Psalms82:6 I said, ""You are gods. You are all sons of the Most High." 
Psalms82:7 You will certainly die like humans and fall like any prince. 
Psalms82:8 Arise, O God! Judge the earth, because all the nations belong to you." 
Psalms83:1 A song; a psalm by Asaph. O God, do not remain silent. Do not turn a deaf ear to me. Do not keep quiet, O God." 
Psalms83:2 Look, your enemies are in an uproar. Those who hate you hold their heads high." 
Psalms83:3 They make plans in secret against your people and plot together against those you treasure. 
Psalms83:4 They say, ""Let's wipe out their nation so that the name of Israel will no longer be remembered.""" 
Psalms83:5 They agree completely on their plan. They form an alliance against you: 
Psalms83:6 the tents from Edom and Ishmael, Moab and Hagar," 
Psalms83:7 Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek, Philistia, along with those who live in Tyre." 
Psalms83:8 Even Assyria has joined them. They helped the descendants of Lot. Selah 
Psalms83:9 Do to them what you did to Midian, to Sisera and Jabin at the Kishon River." 
Psalms83:10 They were destroyed at Endor. They became manure to fertilize the ground. 
Psalms83:11 Treat their influential people as you treated Oreb and Zeeb. Treat all their leaders like Zebah and Zalmunna. 
Psalms83:12 They said, ""Let's take God's pasturelands for ourselves.""" 
Psalms83:13 O my God, blow them away like tumbleweeds, like husks in the wind." 
Psalms83:14 Pursue them with your storms, and terrify them with your windstorms" 
Psalms83:15 the way fire burns a forest and flames set mountains on fire. 
Psalms83:16 Let their faces blush with shame, O Lord, so that they must look to you for help." 
Psalms83:17 Let them be put to shame and terrified forever. Let them die in disgrace 
Psalms83:18 so that they must acknowledge you. Your name is the Lord. You alone are the Most High God of the whole earth.

Psalms86:17 Grant me some proof of your goodness so that those who hate me may see it and be put to shame. You, O Lord, have helped me and comforted me."

Psalms89:6 Who in the skies can compare with the Lord? Who among the heavenly beings is like the Lord? 
Psalms89:7 God is terrifying in the council of the holy ones. He is greater and more awe-inspiring than those who surround him. 
Psalms89:8 O Lord God of Armies, who is like you? Mighty Lord, even your faithfulness surrounds you." 
Psalms89:9 You rule the raging sea. When its waves rise, you quiet them." 
Psalms89:10 You crushed Rahab; it was like a corpse. With your strong arm you scattered your enemies. 
Psalms89:11 The heavens are yours. The earth is also yours. You made the world and everything in it. 
Psalms89:12 You created north and south. Mount Tabor and Mount Hermon sing your name joyfully. 
Psalms89:13 Your arm is mighty. Your hand is strong. Your right hand is lifted high. 
Psalms89:14 Righteousness and justice are the foundations of your throne. Mercy and truth stand in front of you. 
Psalms89:15 Blessed are the people who know how to praise you. They walk in the light of your presence, O Lord." 
Psalms89:16 They find joy in your name all day long. They are joyful in your righteousness 
Psalms89:17 because you are the glory of their strength. By your favor you give us victory. 
Psalms89:18 Our shield belongs to the Lord. Our king belongs to the Holy One of Israel. 
Psalms89:19 Once in a vision you said to your faithful ones: I set a boy above warriors. I have raised up one chosen from the people. 
Psalms89:20 I found my servant David. I anointed him with my holy oil. 
Psalms89:21 My hand is ready to help him. My arm will also give him strength. 
Psalms89:22 No enemy will take him by surprise. No wicked person will mistreat him. 
Psalms89:23 I will crush his enemies in front of him and defeat those who hate him. 
Psalms89:24 My faithfulness and mercy will be with him, and in my name he will be victorious." 
Psalms89:25 I will put his {left} hand on the sea and his right hand on the rivers. 
Psalms89:26 He will call out to me, 'You are my Father, my God, and the rock of my salvation.'" 
Psalms89:27 Yes, I will make him the firstborn. He will be the Most High to the kings of the earth." 
Psalms89:28 My mercy will stay with him forever. My promise to him is unbreakable. 
Psalms89:29 I will make his dynasty endure forever and his throne like the days of heaven. 
Psalms89:30 If his descendants abandon my teachings and do not live by my rules," 
Psalms89:31 if they violate my laws and do not obey my commandments," 
Psalms89:32 then with a rod I will punish their rebellion and their crimes with beatings. 
Psalms89:33 But I will not take my mercy away from him or allow my truth to become a lie. 
Psalms89:34 I will not dishonor my promise or alter my own agreement. 
Psalms89:35 On my holiness I have taken an oath once and for all: I will not lie to David. 
Psalms89:36 His dynasty will last forever. His throne will be in my presence like the sun. 
Psalms89:37 Like the moon his throne will stand firm forever. It will be like a faithful witness in heaven. 
Psalms89:38 But you have despised, rejected, and become angry with your anointed one." 
Psalms89:39 You have refused to recognize the promise to your servant and have thrown his crown into the dirt. 
Psalms89:40 You have broken through all his walls and have laid his fortified cities in ruins. 
Psalms89:41 (Everyone who passed by robbed him. He has become the object of his neighbors' scorn.) 
Psalms89:42 You held the right hand of his enemies high and made all of his adversaries rejoice. 
Psalms89:43 You even took his sword out of his hand and failed to support him in battle. 
Psalms89:44 You put an end to his splendor and hurled his throne to the ground. 
Psalms89:45 You cut short the days of his youth and covered him with shame. Selah 
Psalms89:46 How long, O Lord? Will you hide yourself forever? How long will your anger continue to burn like fire?" 
Psalms89:47 Remember how short my life is! Have you created Adam's descendants for no reason? 
Psalms89:48 Can a mortal go on living and never see death? Who can set himself free from the power of the grave? Selah 
Psalms89:49 Where is the evidence of your mercy, Lord? You swore an oath to David on {the basis of} your faithfulness." 
Psalms89:50 Remember, O Lord, how your servant has been insulted. Remember how I have carried in my heart {the insults} from so many people." 
Psalms89:51 Your enemies insulted {me}. They insulted your Messiah every step he took. 
Psalms89:52 Thank the Lord forever. Amen and amen!

Psalms90:4 Indeed, in your sight a thousand years are like a single day, like yesterday—already past— like an hour in the night." 
Psalms90:5 You sweep mortals away. They are a dream. They sprout again in the morning like cut grass.

Psalms90:7 Indeed, your anger consumes us. Your rage terrifies us." 
Psalms90:8 You have set our sins in front of you. You have put our secret sins in the light of your presence.

Psalms102:3 My days disappear like smoke. My bones burn like hot coals. 
Psalms102:4 My heart is beaten down and withered like grass because I have forgotten about eating. 
Psalms102:5 I am nothing but skin and bones because of my loud groans. 
Psalms102:6 I am like a desert owl, like an owl living in the ruins." 
Psalms102:7 I lie awake. I am like a lonely bird on a rooftop. 
Psalms102:8 All day long my enemies insult me. Those who ridicule me use my name as a curse. 
Psalms102:9 I eat ashes like bread and my tears are mixed with my drink 
Psalms102:10 because of your hostility and anger, because you have picked me up and thrown me away."

Psalms102:22 when nations and kingdoms gather to worship the Lord.

Psalms102:25 Long ago you laid the foundation of the earth. Even the heavens are the works of your hands.

Psalms102:27 But you remain the same, and your life will never end."

Psalms103:4 the one who rescues your life from the pit, the one who crowns you with mercy and compassion," 
Psalms103:5 the one who fills your life with blessings so that you become young again like an eagle. 
Psalms103:6 The Lord does what is right and fair for all who are oppressed. 
Psalms103:7 He let Moses know his ways. He let the Israelites know the things he had done. 
Psalms103:8 The Lord is compassionate, merciful, patient, and always ready to forgive." 
Psalms103:9 He will not always accuse us of wrong or be angry {with us} forever. 
Psalms103:10 He has not treated us as we deserve for our sins or paid us back for our wrongs. 
Psalms103:11 As high as the heavens are above the earth— that is how vast his mercy is toward those who fear him. 
Psalms103:12 As far as the east is from the west— that is how far he has removed our rebellious acts from himself. 
Psalms103:13 As a father has compassion for his children, so the Lord has compassion for those who fear him." 
Psalms103:14 He certainly knows what we are made of. He bears in mind that we are dust. 
Psalms103:15 Human life is as short-lived as grass. It blossoms like a flower in the field. 
Psalms103:16 When the wind blows over the flower, it disappears, and there is no longer any sign of it." 
Psalms103:17 But from everlasting to everlasting, the Lord's mercy is on those who fear him. His righteousness belongs to their children and grandchildren," 
Psalms103:18 to those who are faithful to his promise, to those who remember to follow his guiding principles." 
Psalms103:19 The Lord has set his throne in heaven. His kingdom rules everything. 
Psalms103:20 Praise the Lord, all his angels, you mighty beings who carry out his orders and are ready to obey his spoken orders." 
Psalms103:21 Praise the Lord, all his armies, his servants who carry out his will." 
Psalms103:22 Praise the Lord, all his creatures in all the places of his empire. Praise the Lord, my soul!" 
Psalms104:1 Praise the Lord, my soul! O Lord my God, you are very great. You are clothed with splendor and majesty." 
Psalms104:2 You cover yourself with light as though it were a robe. You stretch out the heavens as though they were curtains. 
Psalms104:3 You lay the beams of your home in the water. You use the clouds for your chariot. You move on the wings of the wind. 
Psalms104:4 You make your angels winds and your servants flames of fire. 
Psalms104:5 You set the earth on its foundations so that it can never be shaken. 
Psalms104:6 You covered the earth with an ocean as though it were a robe. Water stood above the mountains 
Psalms104:7 and fled because of your threat. Water ran away at the sound of your thunder. 
Psalms104:8 The mountains rose and the valleys sank to the place you appointed for them. 
Psalms104:9 Water cannot cross the boundary you set and cannot come back to cover the earth. 
Psalms104:10 You make water gush from springs into valleys. It flows between the mountains. 
Psalms104:11 Every wild animal drinks {from them}. Wild donkeys quench their thirst. 
Psalms104:12 The birds live by the streams. They sing among the branches. 
Psalms104:13 You water the mountains from your home above. You fill the earth with the fruits of your labors. 
Psalms104:14 You make grass grow for cattle and make vegetables for humans to use in order to get food from the ground. 
Psalms104:15 You make wine to cheer human hearts, olive oil to make faces shine, and bread to strengthen human hearts." 
Psalms104:16 The Lord's trees, the cedars in Lebanon which he planted, drink their fill." 
Psalms104:17 Birds build their nests in them. Storks make their homes in fir trees. 
Psalms104:18 The high mountains are for wild goats. The rocks are a refuge for badgers. 
Psalms104:19 He created the moon, which marks the seasons, and the sun, which knows when to set." 
Psalms104:20 He brings darkness, and it is nighttime, when all the wild animals in the forest come out." 
Psalms104:21 The young lions roar for their prey and seek their food from God. 
Psalms104:22 When the sun rises, they gather and lie down in their dens." 
Psalms104:23 Then people go to do their work, to do their tasks until evening." 
Psalms104:24 What a large number of things you have made, O Lord! You made them all by wisdom. The earth is filled with your creatures." 
Psalms104:25 The sea is so big and wide with countless creatures, living things both large and small." 
Psalms104:26 Ships sail on it, and Leviathan, which you made, plays in it." 
Psalms104:27 All of them look to you to give them their food at the right time. 
Psalms104:28 You give it to them, and they gather it up. You open your hand, and they are filled with blessings." 
Psalms104:29 You hide your face, and they are terrified. You take away their breath, and they die and return to dust." 
Psalms104:30 You send out your Spirit, and they are created. You renew the face of the earth." 
Psalms104:31 May the glory of the Lord endure forever. May the Lord find joy in what he has made. 
Psalms104:32 He looks at the earth, and it trembles. He touches the mountains, and they smoke." 
Psalms104:33 I will sing to the Lord throughout my life. I will make music to praise my God as long as I live. 
Psalms104:34 May my thoughts be pleasing to him. I will find joy in the Lord. 
Psalms104:35 May sinners vanish from the world. May there no longer be any wicked people. Praise the Lord, my soul! Hallelujah!" 
Psalms105:1 Give thanks to the Lord. Call on him. Make known among the nations what he has done. 
Psalms105:2 Sing to him. Make music to praise him. Meditate on all the miracles he has performed. 
Psalms105:3 Brag about his holy name. Let the hearts of those who seek the Lord rejoice. 
Psalms105:4 Search for the Lord and his strength. Always seek his presence. 
Psalms105:5 Remember the miracles he performed, the amazing things he did, and the judgments he pronounced," 
Psalms105:6 you descendants of his servant Abraham, you descendants of Jacob, his chosen ones." 
Psalms105:7 He is the Lord our God. His judgments are pronounced throughout the earth. 
Psalms105:8 He always remembers his promise, the word that he commanded for a thousand generations," 
Psalms105:9 the promise that he made to Abraham, and his sworn oath to Isaac." 
Psalms105:10 He confirmed it as a law for Jacob, as an everlasting promise to Israel," 
Psalms105:11 by saying, ""I will give you the land of Canaan. It is your share of the inheritance.""" 
Psalms105:12 While the people of Israel were few in number, a small group of foreigners living in that land," 
Psalms105:13 they wandered from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another." 
Psalms105:14 He didn't permit anyone to oppress them. He warned kings about them: 
Psalms105:15 Do not touch my anointed ones or harm my prophets. 
Psalms105:16 He brought famine to the land. He took away their food supply. 
Psalms105:17 He sent a man ahead of them. He sent Joseph, who was sold as a slave." 
Psalms105:18 They hurt his feet with shackles, and cut into his neck with an iron collar." 
Psalms105:19 The Lord's promise tested him through fiery trials until his prediction came true. 
Psalms105:20 The king sent someone to release him. The ruler of nations set him free. 
Psalms105:21 He made Joseph the master of his palace and the ruler of all his possessions. 
Psalms105:22 Joseph trained the king's officers the way he wanted and taught his respected leaders wisdom. 
Psalms105:23 Then Israel came to Egypt. Jacob lived as a foreigner in the land of Ham. 
Psalms105:24 The Lord made his people grow rapidly in number and stronger than their enemies. 
Psalms105:25 He changed their minds so that they hated his people, and they dealt treacherously with his servants." 
Psalms105:26 He sent his servant Moses, and he sent Aaron, whom he had chosen." 
Psalms105:27 They displayed his miraculous signs among them and did amazing things in the land of Ham. 
Psalms105:28 He sent darkness and made {their land} dark. They did not rebel against his orders. 
Psalms105:29 He turned their water into blood and caused their fish to die. 
Psalms105:30 He made their land swarm with frogs, even in the kings' bedrooms." 
Psalms105:31 He spoke, and swarms of flies and gnats infested their whole territory." 
Psalms105:32 He gave them hail and lightning instead of rain throughout their land. 
Psalms105:33 He struck their grapevines and fig trees and smashed the trees in their territory. 
Psalms105:34 He spoke, and countless locusts and grasshoppers came." 
Psalms105:35 They devoured all the plants in the land. They devoured the crops in the fields. 
Psalms105:36 He killed all the firstborn sons, the first ones born in the land when their fathers were young." 
Psalms105:37 He brought Israel out with silver and gold, and no one among his tribes stumbled." 
Psalms105:38 The Egyptians were terrified of Israel, so they were glad when Israel left." 
Psalms105:39 He spread out a cloud as a protective covering and a fire to light up the night. 
Psalms105:40 The Israelites asked, and he brought them quail and filled them with bread from heaven." 
Psalms105:41 He opened a rock, and water gushed and flowed like a river through the dry places." 
Psalms105:42 He remembered his holy promise to his servant Abraham. 
Psalms105:43 He brought his people out with joy, his chosen ones with a song of joy." 
Psalms105:44 He gave them the lands of {other} nations, and they inherited what others had worked for" 
Psalms105:45 so that they would obey his laws and follow his teachings. Hallelujah! 
Psalms106:1 Hallelujah! Give thanks to the Lord because he is good, because his mercy endures forever." 
Psalms106:2 Who can speak about all the mighty things the Lord has done? Who can announce all the things for which he is worthy of praise? 
Psalms106:3 Blessed are those who defend justice and do what is right at all times. 
Psalms106:4 Remember me, O Lord, when you show favor to your people. Come to help me with your salvation" 
Psalms106:5 so that I may see the prosperity of your chosen ones, find joy in our people's happiness, and brag with the people who belong to you." 
Psalms106:6 We have sinned, and so did our ancestors. We have done wrong. We are guilty." 
Psalms106:7 When our ancestors were in Egypt, they gave no thought to your miracles. They did not remember your numerous acts of mercy, so they rebelled at the sea, the Red Sea." 
Psalms106:8 He saved them because of his reputation so that he could make his mighty power known. 
Psalms106:9 He angrily commanded the Red Sea, and it dried up. He led them through deep water as though it were a desert." 
Psalms106:10 He rescued them from the power of the one who hated them. He rescued them from the enemy. 
Psalms106:11 Water covered their adversaries. Not one Egyptian survived. 
Psalms106:12 Then our ancestors believed what he said. They sang his praise. 
Psalms106:13 They quickly forgot what he did. They did not wait for his advice. 
Psalms106:14 They had an unreasonable desire {for food} in the wilderness. In the desert they tested God. 
Psalms106:15 He gave them what they asked for. He {also} gave them a degenerative disease. 
Psalms106:16 In the camp certain men became envious of Moses. They also became envious of Aaron, the Lord's holy one." 
Psalms106:17 The ground split open and swallowed Dathan. It buried Abiram's followers. 
Psalms106:18 A fire broke out among their followers. Flames burned up wicked people. 
Psalms106:19 At Mount Horeb they made {a statue of} a calf. They worshiped an idol made of metal. 
Psalms106:20 They traded their glorious God for the statue of a bull that eats grass. 
Psalms106:21 They forgot God, their savior, the one who did spectacular things in Egypt," 
Psalms106:22 miracles in the land of Ham, and terrifying things at the Red Sea." 
Psalms106:23 God said he was going to destroy them, but Moses, his chosen one, stood in his way to prevent him from exterminating them." 
Psalms106:24 They refused {to enter} the pleasant land. They did not believe what he said. 
Psalms106:25 They complained in their tents. They did not obey the Lord. 
Psalms106:26 Raising his hand, he swore that he would kill them in the wilderness," 
Psalms106:27 kill their descendants among the nations, and scatter them throughout various lands." 
Psalms106:28 They joined in worshiping the god Baal while they were at Peor, and they ate what was sacrificed to the dead." 
Psalms106:29 They infuriated God by what they did, and a plague broke out among them." 
Psalms106:30 Then Phinehas stood between God and the people, and the plague was stopped." 
Psalms106:31 Because of this, Phinehas was considered righteous forever, throughout every generation." 
Psalms106:32 They made God angry by the water at Meribah. Things turned out badly for Moses because of what they did," 
Psalms106:33 since they made him bitter so that he spoke recklessly. 
Psalms106:34 They did not destroy the people as the Lord had told them. 
Psalms106:35 Instead, they intermarried with other nations. They learned to do what other nations did," 
Psalms106:36 and they worshiped their idols, which became a trap for them." 
Psalms106:37 They sacrificed their sons and daughters to demons. 
Psalms106:38 They shed innocent blood, the blood of their own sons and daughters whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan. The land became polluted with blood." 
Psalms106:39 They became filthy because of what they did. They behaved like prostitutes. 
Psalms106:40 The Lord burned with anger against his own people. He was disgusted with those who belonged to him. 
Psalms106:41 He handed them over to other nations, and those who hated them ruled them." 
Psalms106:42 Their enemies oppressed them and made them subject to their power. 
Psalms106:43 He rescued them many times, but they continued to plot rebellion against him and to sink deeper because of their sin." 
Psalms106:44 He saw that they were suffering when he heard their cry for help. 
Psalms106:45 He remembered his promise to them. In keeping with his rich mercy, he changed his plans." 
Psalms106:46 He let them find compassion from all those who held them captive. 
Psalms106:47 Rescue us, O Lord our God, and gather us from the nations so that we may give thanks to your holy name and make your praise our glory." 
Psalms106:48 Thanks be to the Lord God of Israel from everlasting to everlasting. Let all the people say amen. Hallelujah!

Psalms107:5 They were hungry and thirsty. They began to lose hope. 
Psalms107:6 In their distress they cried out to the Lord. He rescued them from their troubles. 
Psalms107:7 He led them on a road that went straight to an inhabited city. 
Psalms107:8 Let them give thanks to the Lord because of his mercy. He performed his miracles for Adam's descendants. 
Psalms107:9 He gave plenty to drink to those who were thirsty. He filled those who were hungry with good food.

Psalms107:16 He shattered bronze gates and cut iron bars in two.

Psalms107:18 All food was disgusting to them, and they came near death's gates." 
Psalms107:19 In their distress they cried out to the Lord. He saved them from their troubles. 
Psalms107:20 He sent his message and healed them. He rescued them from the grave. 
Psalms107:21 Let them give thanks to the Lord because of his mercy. He performed his miracles for Adam's descendants. 
Psalms107:22 Let them bring songs of thanksgiving as their sacrifice. Let them tell in joyful songs what he has done. 
Psalms107:23 Those who sail on the sea in ships, who do business on the high seas,"

Psalms107:26 The sailors aboard ship rose toward the sky. They plunged into the depths. Their courage melted in {the face of} disaster. 
Psalms107:27 They reeled and staggered like drunks, and all their skills as sailors became useless." 
Psalms107:28 In their distress they cried out to the Lord. He led them from their troubles. 
Psalms107:29 He made the storm calm down, and the waves became still." 
Psalms107:30 The sailors were glad that the storm was quiet. He guided them to the harbor they had longed for. 
Psalms107:31 Let them give thanks to the Lord because of his mercy. He performed his miracles for Adam's descendants. 
Psalms107:32 Let them glorify him when the people are gathered for worship. Let them praise him in the company of respected leaders. 
Psalms107:33 He changes rivers into a desert, springs into thirsty ground," 
Psalms107:34 and fertile ground into a layer of salt because of the wickedness of the people living there. 
Psalms107:35 He changes deserts into lakes and dry ground into springs. 
Psalms107:36 There he settles those who are hungry, and they build cities to live in." 
Psalms107:37 They plant in fields and vineyards that produce crops. 
Psalms107:38 He blesses them, and their numbers multiply, and he does not allow a shortage of cattle." 
Psalms107:39 They became few in number and were humiliated because of oppression, disaster, and sorrow." 
Psalms107:40 He poured contempt on their influential people and made them stumble around in a pathless desert. 
Psalms107:41 But now he lifts needy people high above suffering and makes their families like flocks. 
Psalms107:42 Decent people will see this and rejoice, but all the wicked people will shut their mouths." 
Psalms107:43 Let those who {think} they are wise pay attention to these things so that they may understand the Lord's blessings.

Psalms109:1 For the choir director; a psalm by David. O God, whom I praise, do not turn a deaf ear to me." 
Psalms109:2 Wicked and deceitful people have opened their mouths against me. They speak against me with lying tongues. 
Psalms109:3 They surround me with hateful words. They fight against me for no reason. 
Psalms109:4 In return for my love, they accuse me, but I pray for them." 
Psalms109:5 They reward me with evil instead of good and with hatred instead of love. 
Psalms109:6 {I said,} ""Appoint the evil one to oppose him. Let Satan stand beside him." 
Psalms109:7 When he stands trial, let him be found guilty. Let his prayer be considered sinful." 
Psalms109:8 Let his days be few {in number}. Let someone else take his position. 
Psalms109:9 Let his children become fatherless and his wife a widow. 
Psalms109:10 Let his children wander around and beg. Let them seek help far from their ruined homes. 
Psalms109:11 Let a creditor take everything he owns. Let strangers steal what he has worked for. 
Psalms109:12 Let no one be kind to him anymore. Let no one show any pity to his fatherless children. 
Psalms109:13 Let his descendants be cut off and their family name be wiped out by the next generation. 
Psalms109:14 Let the Lord remember the guilt of his ancestors and not wipe out his mother's sin. 
Psalms109:15 Let their guilt and sin always remain on record in front of the Lord. Let the Lord remove every memory of him from the earth," 
Psalms109:16 because he did not remember to be kind. He drove oppressed, needy, and brokenhearted people to their graves." 
Psalms109:17 He loved to put curses {on others}, so he, too, was cursed. He did not like to bless {others}, so he never received a blessing." 
Psalms109:18 He wore cursing as though it were clothing, so cursing entered his body like water and his bones like oil." 
Psalms109:19 Let cursing be his clothing, a belt he always wears.""" 
Psalms109:20 This is how the Lord rewards those who accuse me, those who say evil things against me." 
Psalms109:21 O Lord Almighty, deal with me out of the goodness of your name. Rescue me because of your mercy." 
Psalms109:22 I am oppressed and needy. I can feel the pain in my heart. 
Psalms109:23 I fade away like a lengthening shadow. I have been shaken off like a grasshopper. 
Psalms109:24 My knees give way because I have been fasting. My body has become lean, without any fat." 
Psalms109:25 I have become the victim of my enemies' insults. They look at me and shake their heads. 
Psalms109:26 Help me, O Lord my God. Save me because of your mercy." 
Psalms109:27 Then they will know that this is your doing, that you, O Lord, are the one who saved me." 
Psalms109:28 They may curse, but you will bless. Let those who attack me be ashamed, but let me rejoice." 
Psalms109:29 Let those who accuse me wear disgrace as though it were clothing. Let them be wrapped in their shame as though it were a robe. 
Psalms109:30 With my mouth I will give many thanks to the Lord. I will praise him among many people," 
Psalms109:31 because he stands beside needy people to save them from those who would condemn them to death. 
Psalms110:1 A psalm by David. The Lord said to my Lord, ""Sit in the highest position in heaven until I make your enemies your footstool.""" 
Psalms110:2 The Lord will extend your powerful scepter from Zion. Rule your enemies who surround you. 
Psalms110:3 Your people will volunteer when you call up your army. Your young people will come to you in holy splendor like dew in the early morning.

Psalms110:5 The Lord is at your right side. He will crush kings on the day of his anger. 
Psalms110:6 He will pass judgment on the nations and fill them with dead bodies. Throughout the earth he will crush {their} heads. 
Psalms110:7 He will drink from the brook along the road. He will hold his head high.

Psalms111:5 He provides food for those who fear him. He always remembers his promise.

Psalms111:7 His works are done with truth and justice. All his guiding principles are trustworthy.

Psalms111:9 He has sent salvation to his people. He has ordered that his promise should continue forever. His name is holy and terrifying. 
Psalms111:10 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom. Good sense is shown by everyone who follows {God's guiding principles}. His praise continues forever.

Psalms112:9 He gives freely to poor people. His righteousness continues forever. His head is raised in honor.

Psalms120:4 He will give you a warrior's sharpened arrows and red-hot coals.

Psalms139:4 Even before there is a {single} word on my tongue, you know all about it, Lord." 
Psalms139:5 You are all around me—in front of me and in back of me. You lay your hand on me.

Psalms139:7 Where can I go {to get away} from your Spirit? Where can I run {to get away} from you? 
Psalms139:8 If I go up to heaven, you are there. If I make my bed in hell, you are there."
