Proverbs25:18 {Like} a club and a sword and a sharp arrow, {so} is a person who gives false testimony against his neighbor." 
Proverbs25:19 {Like} a broken tooth and a lame foot, {so} is confidence in an unfaithful person in a {time of} crisis." 
Proverbs25:20 {Like} taking off a coat on a cold day or pouring vinegar on baking soda, so is singing songs to one who has an evil heart." 
Proverbs25:21 If your enemy is hungry, give him some food to eat, and if he is thirsty, give him some water to drink." 
Proverbs25:22 {In this way} you will make him feel guilty and ashamed, and the Lord will reward you." 
Proverbs25:23 {As} the north wind brings rain, so a whispering tongue brings angry looks." 
Proverbs25:24 Better to live on a corner of a roof than to share a home with a quarreling woman. 
Proverbs25:25 {Like} cold water to a thirsty soul, so is good news from far away." 
Proverbs25:26 {Like} a muddied spring and a polluted well, {so} is a righteous person who gives in to a wicked person." 
Proverbs25:27 Eating too much honey is not good, and searching for honor is not honorable." 
Proverbs25:28 {Like} a city broken into {and} left without a wall, {so} is a person who lacks self-control." 
Proverbs26:1 Like snow in summertime and rain at harvest time, so honor is not right for a fool." 
Proverbs26:2 Like a fluttering sparrow, like a darting swallow, so a hastily spoken curse does not come to rest." 
Proverbs26:3 A whip is for the horse, a bridle is for the donkey, and a rod is for the backs of fools." 
Proverbs26:4 Do not answer a fool with his own stupidity, or you will be like him." 
Proverbs26:5 Answer a fool with his own stupidity, or he will think he is wise." 
Proverbs26:6 Whoever uses a fool to send a message cuts off his own feet and brings violence upon himself. 
Proverbs26:7 {Like} a lame person's limp legs, so is a proverb in the mouths of fools." 
Proverbs26:8 Like tying a stone to a sling, so is giving honor to a fool." 
Proverbs26:9 {Like} a thorn stuck in a drunk's hand, so is a proverb in the mouths of fools." 
Proverbs26:10 {Like} many people who destroy everything, so is one who hires fools or drifters." 
Proverbs26:11 As a dog goes back to its vomit, {so} a fool repeats his stupidity." 
Proverbs26:12 Have you met a person who thinks he is wise? There is more hope for a fool than for him. 
Proverbs26:13 A lazy person says, ""There's a ferocious lion out on the road! There's a lion loose in the streets!""" 
Proverbs26:14 {As} a door turns on its hinges, so the lazy person turns on his bed." 
Proverbs26:15 A lazy person puts his fork in his food. He wears himself out as he brings it back to his mouth. 
Proverbs26:16 A lazy person thinks he is wiser than seven people who give a sensible answer. 
Proverbs26:17 {Like} grabbing a dog by the ears, {so} is a bystander who gets involved in someone else's quarrel." 
Proverbs26:18 Like a madman who shoots flaming arrows, arrows, and death," 
Proverbs26:19 so is the person who tricks his neighbor and says, ""I was only joking!""" 
Proverbs26:20 Without wood a fire goes out, and without gossip a quarrel dies down." 
Proverbs26:21 {As} charcoal fuels burning coals and wood fuels fire, so a quarrelsome person fuels a dispute." 
Proverbs26:22 The words of a gossip are swallowed greedily, and they go down into a person's innermost being." 
Proverbs26:23 {Like} a clay pot covered with cheap silver, {so} is smooth talk that covers up an evil heart." 
Proverbs26:24 Whoever is filled with hate disguises it with his speech, but inside he holds on to deceit." 
Proverbs26:25 When he talks charmingly, do not trust him because of the seven disgusting things in his heart." 
Proverbs26:26 His hatred is deceitfully hidden, but his wickedness will be revealed to the community." 
Proverbs26:27 Whoever digs a pit will fall into it. Whoever rolls a stone will have it roll back on him. 
Proverbs26:28 A lying tongue hates its victims, and a flattering mouth causes ruin." 
Proverbs27:1 Do not brag about tomorrow, because you do not know what another day may bring." 
Proverbs27:2 Praise should come from another person and not from your own mouth, from a stranger and not from your own lips." 
Proverbs27:3 A stone is heavy, and sand weighs a lot, but annoyance caused by a stubborn fool is heavier than both." 
Proverbs27:4 Anger is cruel, and fury is overwhelming, but who can survive jealousy?" 
Proverbs27:5 Open criticism is better than unexpressed love. 
Proverbs27:6 Wounds made by a friend are intended to help, but an enemy's kisses are too much to bear." 
Proverbs27:7 One who is full despises honey, but to one who is hungry, even bitter food tastes sweet." 
Proverbs27:8 Like a bird wandering from its nest, so is a husband wandering from his home." 
Proverbs27:9 Perfume and incense make the heart glad, but the sweetness of a friend is a fragrant forest." 
Proverbs27:10 Do not abandon your friend or your father's friend. Do not go to a relative's home when you are in trouble. A neighbor living nearby is better than a relative far away. 
Proverbs27:11 Be wise, my son, and make my heart glad so that I can answer anyone who criticizes me." 
Proverbs27:12 Sensible people foresee trouble and hide. Gullible people go ahead {and} suffer. 
Proverbs27:13 Hold on to the garment of one who guarantees a stranger's loan, and hold responsible the person who makes a loan in behalf of a foreigner." 
Proverbs27:14 Whoever blesses his friend early in the morning with a loud voice— his blessing is considered a curse. 
Proverbs27:15 Constantly dripping water on a rainy day is like a quarreling woman. 
Proverbs27:16 Whoever can control her can control the wind. He can even pick up olive oil with his right hand. 
Proverbs27:17 {As} iron sharpens iron, so one person sharpens the wits of another." 
Proverbs27:18 Whoever takes care of a fig tree can eat its fruit, and whoever protects his master is honored." 
Proverbs27:19 As a face is reflected in water, so a person is reflected by his heart." 
Proverbs27:20 Hell and decay are never satisfied, and a person's eyes are never satisfied." 
Proverbs27:21 The crucible is for refining silver and the smelter for gold, but a person {is tested} by the praise given to him." 
Proverbs27:22 If you crush a stubborn fool in a mortar with a pestle along with grain, {even then} his stupidity will not leave him." 
Proverbs27:23 Be fully aware of the condition of your flock, and pay close attention to your herds." 
Proverbs27:24 Wealth is not forever. Nor does a crown last from one generation to the next. 
Proverbs27:25 {When} grass is cut short, the tender growth appears, and vegetables are gathered on the hills." 
Proverbs27:26 Lambs {will provide} you with clothing, and the money from the male goats will buy a field." 
Proverbs27:27 There will be enough goat milk to feed you, to feed your family, and to keep your servant girls alive." 
Proverbs28:1 A wicked person flees when no one is chasing him, but righteous people are as bold as lions." 
Proverbs28:2 When a country is in revolt, it has many rulers, but only with a person who has understanding and knowledge will it last a long time." 
Proverbs28:3 A poor person who oppresses poorer people is like a driving rain that leaves no food. 
Proverbs28:4 Those who abandon {God's} teachings praise wicked people, but those who follow {God's} teachings oppose wicked people." 
Proverbs28:5 Evil people do not understand justice, but those who seek the Lord understand everything." 
Proverbs28:6 Better to be a poor person who has integrity than to be rich and double-dealing. 
Proverbs28:7 Whoever follows {God's} teachings is a wise son. Whoever associates with gluttons disgraces his father. 
Proverbs28:8 Whoever becomes wealthy through {unfair} loans and interest collects them for the one who is kind to the poor. 
Proverbs28:9 Surely the prayer of someone who refuses to listen to {God's} teachings is disgusting. 
Proverbs28:10 Whoever leads decent people into evil will fall into his own pit, but innocent people will inherit good things." 
Proverbs28:11 A rich person is wise in his own eyes, but a poor person with understanding sees right through him." 
Proverbs28:12 When righteous people triumph, there is great glory, but when wicked people rise, people hide themselves." 
Proverbs28:13 Whoever covers over his sins does not prosper. Whoever confesses and abandons them receives compassion. 
Proverbs28:14 Blessed is the one who is always fearful {of sin}, but whoever is hard-hearted falls into disaster." 
Proverbs28:15 {Like} a roaring lion and a charging bear, {so} a wicked ruler is a threat to poor people." 
Proverbs28:16 A leader without understanding taxes {his people} heavily, but those who hate unjust gain will live longer." 
Proverbs28:17 A person burdened with the guilt of murder will be a fugitive down to his grave. No one will help him. 
Proverbs28:18 Whoever lives honestly will be safe. Whoever lives dishonestly will fall all at once. 
Proverbs28:19 Whoever works his land will have plenty to eat. Whoever chases unrealistic dreams will have plenty of nothing. 
Proverbs28:20 A trustworthy person has many blessings, but anyone in a hurry to get rich will not escape punishment." 
Proverbs28:21 Showing partiality is not good, because some people will turn on you even for a piece of bread." 
Proverbs28:22 A stingy person is in a hurry to get rich, not realizing that poverty is about to overtake him." 
Proverbs28:23 Whoever criticizes people will be more highly regarded in the future than the one who flatters with his tongue. 
Proverbs28:24 The one who robs his father or his mother and says, ""It isn't wrong!"" is a companion to a vandal." 
Proverbs28:25 A greedy person stirs up a fight, but whoever trusts the Lord prospers." 
Proverbs28:26 Whoever trusts his own heart is a fool. Whoever walks in wisdom will survive. 
Proverbs28:27 Whoever gives to the poor lacks nothing. Whoever ignores the poor receives many curses. 
Proverbs28:28 When wicked people rise, people hide. When they die, righteous people increase." 
Proverbs29:1 A person who will not bend after many warnings will suddenly be broken beyond repair. 
Proverbs29:2 When righteous people increase, the people {of God} rejoice, but when a wicked person rules, everybody groans." 
Proverbs29:3 A person who loves wisdom makes his father happy, but one who pays prostitutes wastes his wealth." 
Proverbs29:4 By means of justice, a king builds up a country, but a person who confiscates religious contributions tears it down." 
Proverbs29:5 A person who flatters his neighbor is spreading a net for him to step into. 
Proverbs29:6 To an evil person sin is bait in a trap, but a righteous person runs away from it and is glad." 
Proverbs29:7 A righteous person knows the just cause of the poor. A wicked person does not understand this. 
Proverbs29:8 Mockers create an uproar in a city, but wise people turn away anger." 
Proverbs29:9 When a wise person goes to court with a stubborn fool, he may rant and rave, but there is no peace and quiet." 
Proverbs29:10 Bloodthirsty people hate an innocent person, but decent people seek {to protect} his life." 
Proverbs29:11 A fool expresses all his emotions, but a wise person controls them." 
Proverbs29:12 If a ruler pays attention to lies, all his servants become wicked." 
Proverbs29:13 A poor person and an oppressor have this in common: The Lord gives both of them sight. 
Proverbs29:14 When a king judges the poor with honesty, his throne will always be secure." 
Proverbs29:15 A spanking and a warning produce wisdom, but an undisciplined child disgraces his mother." 
Proverbs29:16 When wicked people increase, crime increases, but righteous people will witness their downfall." 
Proverbs29:17 Correct your son, and he will give you peace of mind. He will bring delight to your soul." 
Proverbs29:18 Without prophetic vision people run wild, but blessed are those who follow {God's} teachings." 
Proverbs29:19 A slave cannot be disciplined with words. He will not respond, though he may understand." 
Proverbs29:20 Have you met a person who is quick to answer? There is more hope for a fool than for him. 
Proverbs29:21 Pamper a slave from childhood, and later he will be ungrateful." 
Proverbs29:22 An angry person stirs up a fight, and a hothead does much wrong." 
Proverbs29:23 A person's pride will humiliate him, but a humble spirit gains honor." 
Proverbs29:24 Whoever is a thief's partner hates his own life. He will not testify under oath. 
Proverbs29:25 A person's fear sets a trap {for him}, but one who trusts the Lord is safe." 
Proverbs29:26 Many seek an audience with a ruler, but justice for humanity comes from the Lord." 
Proverbs29:27 An unjust person is disgusting to righteous people. A decent person is disgusting to wicked people. 
Proverbs30:1 The words of Agur, son of Jakeh. Agur's prophetic revelation. To God This man's declaration: ""I'm weary, O God. I'm weary and worn out, O God." 
Proverbs30:2 I'm more {like} a dumb animal than a human being. I don't {even} have human understanding. 
Proverbs30:3 I haven't learned wisdom. I don't have knowledge of the Holy One. 
Proverbs30:4 To the audience Who has gone up to heaven and come down? Who has gathered the wind in the palm of his hand? Who has wrapped water in a garment? Who has set up the earth from one end to the other? What is his name or the name of his son? Certainly, you must know!" 
Proverbs30:5 Every word of God has proven to be true. He is a shield to those who come to him for protection. 
Proverbs30:6 Do not add to his words, or he will reprimand you, and you will be found to be a liar." 
Proverbs30:7 To God I've asked you for two things. Don't keep them from me before I die: 
Proverbs30:8 Keep vanity and lies far away from me. Don't give me either poverty or riches. Feed me {only} the food I need," 
Proverbs30:9 or I may feel satisfied and deny you and say, 'Who is the Lord?' or I may become poor and steal and give the name of my God a bad reputation." 
Proverbs30:10 To the audience Do not slander a slave to his master. The slave will curse you, and you will be found guilty.""" 
Proverbs30:11 A certain kind of person curses his father and does not bless his mother. 
Proverbs30:12 A certain kind of person thinks he is pure but is not washed from his own feces. 
Proverbs30:13 A certain kind of person looks around arrogantly and is conceited. 
Proverbs30:14 A certain kind of person, whose teeth are like swords and whose jaws are {like} knives, devours oppressed people from the earth and people from among humanity." 
Proverbs30:15 The bloodsucking leech has two daughters—Give! and Give! Three things are never satisfied. Four never say, ""Enough!"":" 
Proverbs30:16 the grave, a barren womb, a land that never gets enough water, a fire that does not say, ""Enough!""" 
Proverbs30:17 The eye that makes fun of a father and hates to obey a mother will be plucked out by ravens in the valley and eaten by young vultures. 
Proverbs30:18 Three things are too amazing to me, even four that I cannot understand:" 
Proverbs30:19 an eagle making its way through the sky, a snake making its way over a rock, a ship making its way through high seas, a man making his way with a virgin." 
Proverbs30:20 This is the way of a woman who commits adultery: She eats, wipes her mouth, and says, ""I haven't done anything wrong!""" 
Proverbs30:21 Three things cause the earth to tremble, even four it cannot bear up under:" 
Proverbs30:22 a slave when he becomes king, a godless fool when he is filled with food," 
Proverbs30:23 a woman who is unloved when she gets married, a maid when she replaces her mistress." 
Proverbs30:24 Four things on earth are small, yet they are very wise:" 
Proverbs30:25 Ants are not a strong species, yet they store their food in summer." 
Proverbs30:26 Rock badgers are not a mighty species, yet they make their home in the rocks." 
Proverbs30:27 Locusts have no king, yet all of them divide into swarms by instinct." 
Proverbs30:28 A lizard you can hold in your hands, yet it can even be found in royal palaces." 
Proverbs30:29 There are three things that walk with dignity, even four that march with dignity:" 
Proverbs30:30 a lion, mightiest among animals, which turns away from nothing," 
Proverbs30:31 a strutting rooster, a male goat, a king at the head of his army." 
Proverbs30:32 If you are such a godless fool as to honor yourself, or if you scheme, you had better put your hand over your mouth." 
Proverbs30:33 As churning milk produces butter and punching a nose produces blood, so stirring up anger produces a fight." 
Proverbs31:1 The sayings of King Lemuel, a prophetic revelation, used by his mother to discipline him." 
Proverbs31:2 What, my son? What, son to whom I gave birth? What, son of my prayers?" 
Proverbs31:3 Don't give your strength to women or your power to those who ruin kings. 
Proverbs31:4 It is not for kings, Lemuel. It is not for kings to drink wine or for rulers to crave liquor." 
Proverbs31:5 Otherwise, they drink and forget what they have decreed and change the standard of justice for all oppressed people." 
Proverbs31:6 Give liquor to a person who is dying and wine to one who feels resentful. 
Proverbs31:7 Such a person drinks and forgets his poverty and does not remember his trouble anymore. 
Proverbs31:8 Speak out for the one who cannot speak, for the rights of those who are doomed." 
Proverbs31:9 Speak out, judge fairly, and defend the rights of oppressed and needy people.""" 
Proverbs31:10 Who can find a wife with a strong character? She is worth far more than jewels. 
Proverbs31:11 Her husband trusts her with {all} his heart, and he does not lack anything good." 
Proverbs31:12 She helps him and never harms him all the days of her life. 
Proverbs31:13 She seeks out wool and linen {with care} and works with willing hands. 
Proverbs31:14 She is like merchant ships. She brings her food from far away. 
Proverbs31:15 She wakes up while it is still dark and gives food to her family and portions of food to her female slaves. 
Proverbs31:16 She picks out a field and buys it. She plants a vineyard from the profits she has earned. 
Proverbs31:17 She puts on strength like a belt and goes to work with energy. 
Proverbs31:18 She sees that she is making a good profit. Her lamp burns late at night. 
Proverbs31:19 She puts her hands on the distaff, and her fingers hold a spindle." 
Proverbs31:20 She opens her hands to oppressed people and stretches them out to needy people. 
Proverbs31:21 She does not fear for her family when it snows because her whole family has a double layer of clothing. 
Proverbs31:22 She makes quilts for herself. Her clothes are {made of} linen and purple cloth. 
Proverbs31:23 Her husband is known at the city gates when he sits with the leaders of the land. 
Proverbs31:24 She makes linen garments and sells them and delivers belts to the merchants. 
Proverbs31:25 She dresses with strength and nobility, and she smiles at the future." 
Proverbs31:26 She speaks with wisdom, and on her tongue there is tender instruction." 
Proverbs31:27 She keeps a close eye on the conduct of her family, and she does not eat the bread of idleness." 
Proverbs31:28 Her children and her husband stand up and bless her. In addition, he sings her praises, by saying," 
Proverbs31:29 'Many women have done noble work, but you have surpassed them all!'" 
Proverbs31:30 Charm is deceptive, and beauty evaporates, {but} a woman who has the fear of the Lord should be praised." 
Proverbs31:31 Reward her for what she has done, and let her achievements praise her at the city gates.""" 
Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the spokesman, the son of David and the king in Jerusalem." 
Ecclesiastes1:2 Absolutely pointless! says the spokesman. Absolutely pointless! Everything is pointless. 
Ecclesiastes1:3 What do people gain from all their hard work under the sun? 
Ecclesiastes1:4 Generations come, and generations go, but the earth lasts forever." 
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun rises, and the sun sets, and then it rushes back to the place where it will rise {again}." 
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind blows toward the south and shifts toward the north. Round and round it blows. It blows in a full circle. 
Ecclesiastes1:7 All streams flow into the sea, but the sea is never full. The water goes back to the place where the streams began in order to {start} flowing again." 
Ecclesiastes1:8 All of these sayings are worn-out phrases. They are more than anyone can express, comprehend, or understand." 
Ecclesiastes1:9 Whatever has happened before will happen {again}. Whatever has been done before will be done {again}. There is nothing new under the sun. 
Ecclesiastes1:10 Can you say that anything is new? It has already been here long before us. 
Ecclesiastes1:11 Nothing from the past is remembered. Even in the future, nothing will be remembered by those who come after us." 
Ecclesiastes1:12 I, the spokesman, have been king of Israel in Jerusalem." 
Ecclesiastes1:13 With all my heart I used wisdom to study and explore everything done under heaven. Mortals are weighed down with a terrible burden that God has placed on them. 
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen everything that is done under the sun. Look at it! It's all pointless. {It's like} trying to catch the wind. 
Ecclesiastes1:15 No one can straighten what is bent. No one can count what is not there. 
Ecclesiastes1:16 I thought to myself, ""I have grown wiser than anyone who {has ruled} Jerusalem before me. I've had a lot of experience with wisdom and knowledge.""" 
Ecclesiastes1:17 I've used my mind to understand wisdom and knowledge as well as madness and stupidity. {Now} I know that this is {like} trying to catch the wind. 
Ecclesiastes1:18 With a lot of wisdom {comes} a lot of heartache. The greater {your} knowledge, the greater {your} pain." 
Ecclesiastes2:1 I thought to myself, ""Now I want to experiment with pleasure and enjoy myself."" But even this was pointless." 
Ecclesiastes2:2 I thought, ""Laughter doesn't make any sense. What does pleasure accomplish?""" 
Ecclesiastes2:3 I explored ways to make myself feel better by drinking wine. I also explored ways to do {some} foolish things. During all that time, wisdom continued to control my mind. I was able to determine whether this was good for mortals to do during their brief lives under heaven." 
Ecclesiastes2:4 I accomplished some great things: I built houses for myself. I planted vineyards for myself. 
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made gardens and parks for myself. I planted every kind of fruit tree in them. 
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made pools to water the forest of growing trees. 
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought male and female slaves. In addition, slaves were born in my household. I owned more herds and flocks than anyone in Jerusalem before me." 
Ecclesiastes2:8 I also gathered silver and gold for myself. I gathered the treasures of kings and provinces. I provided myself with male and female singers and the pleasures men have with one concubine after another. 
Ecclesiastes2:9 So I grew richer than anyone in Jerusalem before me. Yet, my wisdom remained with me." 
Ecclesiastes2:10 If something appealed to me, I did it. I allowed myself to have any pleasure I wanted, since I found pleasure in my work. This was my reward for all my hard work." 
Ecclesiastes2:11 But when I turned to look at all that I had accomplished and all the hard work I had put into it, I saw that it was all pointless. {It was like} trying to catch the wind. I gained nothing {from any of my accomplishments} under the sun." 
Ecclesiastes2:12 Then I turned {my attention} to experience wisdom, madness, and foolishness. For instance, what can the man who replaces the king do? Only what has already been done." 
Ecclesiastes2:13 But I saw that wisdom has an advantage over foolishness as light has an advantage over darkness. 
Ecclesiastes2:14 A wise person uses the eyes in his head, but a fool walks in the dark. But I have also come to realize that the same destiny waits for both of them." 
Ecclesiastes2:15 I thought to myself, ""{If} the destiny that waits for the fool waits for me as well, then what is the advantage in being wise?"" So I thought that even this is pointless." 
Ecclesiastes2:16 Neither the wise person nor the fool will be remembered for long, since both will be forgotten in the days to come. Both the wise person and the fool will die." 
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I came to hate life because everything done under the sun seemed wrong to me. Everything was pointless. {It was like} trying to catch the wind. 
Ecclesiastes2:18 I came to hate everything for which I had worked so hard under the sun, because I will have to leave it to the person who replaces me." 
Ecclesiastes2:19 Who knows whether that person will be wise or foolish? He will still have control over everything under the sun for which I worked so hard and used my wisdom. Even this is pointless. 
Ecclesiastes2:20 Then I fell into despair over everything for which I had worked so hard under the sun. 
Ecclesiastes2:21 Here is someone who had worked hard with wisdom, knowledge, and skill. Yet, he must turn over his estate to someone else, who didn't work for it. Even this is pointless and a terrible tragedy." 
Ecclesiastes2:22 What do people get from all of their hard work and struggles under the sun? 
Ecclesiastes2:23 Their entire life is filled with pain, and their work is unbearable. Even at night their minds don't rest. Even this is pointless." 
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for people to do than to eat, drink, and find satisfaction in their work. I saw that even this comes from the hand of God." 
Ecclesiastes2:25 Who can eat or enjoy themselves without God? 
Ecclesiastes2:26 God gives wisdom, knowledge, and joy to anyone who pleases him. But to the person who continues to sin, he gives the job of gathering and collecting {wealth}. The sinner must turn his wealth over to the person who pleases God. Even this is pointless. {It's like} trying to catch the wind." 
Ecclesiastes3:1 Everything has its own time, and there is a specific time for every activity under heaven:" 
Ecclesiastes3:2 a time to be born and a time to die, a time to plant and a time to pull out what was planted," 
Ecclesiastes3:3 a time to kill and a time to heal, a time to tear down and a time to build up," 
Ecclesiastes3:4 a time to cry and a time to laugh, a time to mourn and a time to dance," 
Ecclesiastes3:5 a time to scatter stones and a time to gather them, a time to hug and a time to stop hugging," 
Ecclesiastes3:6 a time to start looking and a time to stop looking, a time to keep and a time to throw away," 
Ecclesiastes3:7 a time to tear apart and a time to sew together, a time to keep quiet and a time to speak out," 
Ecclesiastes3:8 a time to love and a time to hate, a time for war and a time for peace." 
Ecclesiastes3:9 What do working people gain from their hard labor? 
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen mortals weighed down with a burden that God has placed on them. 
Ecclesiastes3:11 It is beautiful how God has done everything at the right time. He has put a sense of eternity in people's minds. Yet, mortals still can't grasp what God is doing from the beginning to the end {of time}." 
Ecclesiastes3:12 I realize that there's nothing better for them to do than to be cheerful and enjoy what is good in their lives. 
Ecclesiastes3:13 It is a gift from God to be able to eat and drink and experience the good that comes from every kind of hard work. 
Ecclesiastes3:14 I realize that whatever God does will last forever. Nothing can be added to it, and nothing can be taken away from it. God does this so that people will fear him." 
Ecclesiastes3:15 Whatever has happened {in the past} is present now. Whatever is going to happen {in the future} has already happened {in the past}. God will call the past to account. 
Ecclesiastes3:16 I saw something else under the sun: There is wickedness where justice should be found. There is wickedness where righteousness should be found. 
Ecclesiastes3:17 I thought to myself, ""God will judge righteous people as well as wicked people, because there is a specific time for every activity and every work that is done.""" 
Ecclesiastes3:18 I thought to myself, ""God is going to test humans in order to show them that they are {like} animals.""" 
Ecclesiastes3:19 Humans and animals have the same destiny. One dies just like the other. All of them have the same breath {of life}. Humans have no advantage over animals. All {of life} is pointless. 
Ecclesiastes3:20 All {life} goes to the same place. All {life} comes from the ground, and all of it goes back to the ground." 
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows whether a human spirit goes upward or whether an animal spirit goes downward to the earth? 
Ecclesiastes3:22 I saw that there's nothing better for people to do than to enjoy their work because that is their lot {in life}. Who will allow them to see what will happen after them? 
Ecclesiastes4:1 Next, I turned to look at all the acts of oppression that make people suffer under the sun. Look at the tears of those who suffer! No one can comfort them. Their oppressors have {all} the power. No one can comfort those who suffer." 
Ecclesiastes4:2 I congratulate the dead, who have already died, rather than the living, who still have to carry on." 
Ecclesiastes4:3 But the person who hasn't been born yet is better off than both of them. He hasn't seen the evil that is done under the sun. 
Ecclesiastes4:4 Then I saw that all hard work and skillful effort come from rivalry. Even this is pointless. {It's like} trying to catch the wind. 
Ecclesiastes4:5 A fool folds his hands and wastes away. 
Ecclesiastes4:6 One handful of peace and quiet is better than two handfuls of hard work and of trying to catch the wind. 
Ecclesiastes4:7 Next, I turned to look at something pointless under the sun:" 
Ecclesiastes4:8 There are people who are all alone. They have no children or other family members. So there is no end to all the hard work they have to do. Their eyes are never satisfied with riches. But {they never ask themselves} why they are working so hard and depriving themselves of good things. Even this is pointless and a terrible tragedy. 
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two people are better than one because {together} they have a good reward for their hard work. 
Ecclesiastes4:10 If one falls, the other can help his friend get up. But how tragic it is for the one who is {all} alone when he falls. There is no one to help him get up." 
Ecclesiastes4:11 Again, if two people lie down together, they can keep warm, but how can one person keep warm?" 
Ecclesiastes4:12 Though one person may be overpowered by another, two people can resist one opponent. A triple-braided rope is not easily broken." 
Ecclesiastes4:13 A young man who is poor and wise is better than an old, foolish king who won't take advice any longer." 
Ecclesiastes4:14 A young man came out of prison to rule as king, even though he had been born in poverty in that same kingdom." 
Ecclesiastes4:15 I saw all living people moving about under the sun. They sided with the second young man, the king's successor." 
Ecclesiastes4:16 There was no end to all those people, everyone whom he led. But those who will come later will not be happy with the successor. Even this is pointless. {It's like} trying to catch the wind." 
Ecclesiastes5:1 Watch your step when you go to the house of God. It is better to go there and listen than to bring the sacrifices fools bring. Fools are unaware that they are doing {something} evil. 
Ecclesiastes5:2 Don't be in a hurry to talk. Don't be eager to speak in the presence of God. Since God is in heaven and you are on earth, limit the number of your words." 
Ecclesiastes5:3 Daydreaming comes when there are too many worries. Careless speaking comes when there are too many words. 
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you make a promise to God, don't be slow to keep it because God doesn't like fools. Keep your promise." 
Ecclesiastes5:5 It is better not to make a promise than to make one and not keep it. 
Ecclesiastes5:6 Don't let your mouth talk you into committing a sin. Don't say in the presence of a {temple} messenger, ""My promise was a mistake!"" Why should God become angry at your excuse and destroy what you've accomplished?" 
Ecclesiastes5:7 In spite of many daydreams, pointless actions, and empty words, you should still fear God." 
Ecclesiastes5:8 Don't be surprised if you see poor people being oppressed, denied justice, or denied their rights in any district. One authority is watching over another, and they both have authorities watching over them." 
Ecclesiastes5:9 Yet, a king is an advantage for a country with cultivated fields." 
Ecclesiastes5:10 Whoever loves money will never be satisfied with money. Whoever loves wealth will never be satisfied with more income. Even this is pointless. 
Ecclesiastes5:11 As the number of goods increase, so do the number of people who consume them. What do owners gain {from all their goods} except {the opportunity} to look at them?" 
Ecclesiastes5:12 The sleep of working people is sweet, whether they eat a little or a lot. But the full stomachs that rich people have will not allow them to sleep." 
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a painful tragedy that I have seen under the sun: Riches lead to the downfall of those who hoard them. 
Ecclesiastes5:14 These hoarded riches were then lost in bad business deals. The owners had children, but now they have nothing to give them." 
Ecclesiastes5:15 They came from their mother's womb naked. They will leave as naked as they came. They won't even be able to take a handful of their earnings with them from all their hard work. 
Ecclesiastes5:16 This also is a painful tragedy: They leave exactly as they came. What advantage do they gain from working so hard for the wind? 
Ecclesiastes5:17 They spend their entire lives in darkness, in constant frustration, sickness, and resentment." 
Ecclesiastes5:18 At last I have seen what is good and beautiful: It is to eat and drink and to enjoy the good in all our hard work under the sun during the brief lives God gives us. That is our lot {in life}. 
Ecclesiastes5:19 It is a gift from God when God gives some people wealth and possessions, the power to enjoy them, {the ability} to accept their lot in life, and {the ability} to rejoice in their own hard work." 
Ecclesiastes5:20 These people won't give much thought to their brief lives because God keeps them occupied with the joy in their hearts. 
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is a tragedy that I have seen under the sun. It is a terrible one for mortals. 
Ecclesiastes6:2 God gives one person riches, wealth, and honor so that he doesn't lack anything he wants. Yet, God doesn't give him the power to enjoy any of them. Instead, a stranger enjoys them. This is pointless and is a painful tragedy." 
Ecclesiastes6:3 Suppose a rich person wasn't satisfied with good things {while he was alive} and didn't even get an honorable burial {after he died}. Suppose he had a hundred children and lived for many years. No matter how long he would have lived, it {still} would have been better for him to have been born dead." 
Ecclesiastes6:4 A stillborn baby arrives in a pointless birth and goes out into the darkness. The darkness then hides its name. 
Ecclesiastes6:5 Though it has never seen the sun or known anything, the baby finds more rest than the rich person." 
Ecclesiastes6:6 Even if the rich person lives two thousand years without experiencing anything good—don't we all go to the same place? 
Ecclesiastes6:7 Everything that people work so hard for goes into their mouths, but their appetite is never satisfied." 
Ecclesiastes6:8 What advantage does a wise person have over a fool? What advantage does a poor person have in knowing how to face life? 
Ecclesiastes6:9 It is better to look at what is in front of you than to go looking for what you want. Even this is pointless. {It's like} trying to catch the wind. 
Ecclesiastes6:10 Whatever has happened {in the past} already has a name. Mortals are already known for what they are. Mortals cannot argue with the one who is stronger than they. 
Ecclesiastes6:11 The more words there are, the more pointless they become. What advantage do mortals gain from this?" 
Ecclesiastes6:12 Who knows what may be good for mortals while they are alive, during the brief, pointless days they live? Mortals pass by like a shadow. Who will tell them about their future under the sun?" 
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than expensive perfume, and the day you die is better than the day you're born." 
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go to a funeral than to a banquet because that is where everyone will end up. Everyone who is alive should take this to heart! 
Ecclesiastes7:3 Sorrow is better than laughter because, in spite of a sad face, the heart can be joyful." 
Ecclesiastes7:4 The minds of wise people think about funerals, but the minds of fools think about banquets." 
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better to listen to wise people who reprimand you than to fools who sing your praises. 
Ecclesiastes7:6 The laughter of a fool is like the crackling of thorns burning under a pot. Even this is pointless. 
Ecclesiastes7:7 Oppression can turn a wise person into a fool, and a bribe can corrupt the mind." 
Ecclesiastes7:8 The end of something is better than its beginning. It is better to be patient than arrogant. 
Ecclesiastes7:9 Don't be quick to get angry, because anger is typical of fools." 
Ecclesiastes7:10 Don't ask, ""Why were things better in the old days than they are now?"" It isn't wisdom that leads you to ask this!" 
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom is as good as an inheritance. It is an advantage to everyone who sees the sun. 
Ecclesiastes7:12 Wisdom protects us just as money protects us, but the advantage of wisdom is that it gives life to those who have it." 
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider what God has done! Who can straighten what God has bent? 
Ecclesiastes7:14 When times are good, be happy. But when times are bad, consider this: God has made the one time as well as the other so that mortals cannot predict their future." 
Ecclesiastes7:15 I have seen it all in my pointless life: Righteous people die in spite of being righteous. Wicked people go on living in spite of being wicked. 
Ecclesiastes7:16 Don't be too virtuous, and don't be too wise. Why make yourself miserable?" 
Ecclesiastes7:17 Don't be too wicked, and don't be a fool. Why should you die before your time is up?" 
Ecclesiastes7:18 It's good to hold on to the one and not let go of the other, because the one who fears God will be able to avoid both extremes." 
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom will help a wise person more than ten rulers can help a city. 
Ecclesiastes7:20 Certainly, there is no one so righteous on earth that he always does what is good and never sins." 
Ecclesiastes7:21 Don't take everything that people say to heart, or you may hear your own servant cursing you." 
Ecclesiastes7:22 Your conscience knows that you have cursed others many times. 
Ecclesiastes7:23 I used wisdom to test all of this. I said, ""I want to be wise, but it is out of my reach.""" 
Ecclesiastes7:24 Whatever wisdom may be, it is out of reach. It is deep, very deep. Who can find out what it is?" 
Ecclesiastes7:25 I turned my attention to study, to explore, and to seek out wisdom and the reason for things. I learned that wickedness is stupid and foolishness is madness." 
Ecclesiastes7:26 I find that a woman whose thoughts are {like} traps and snares is more bitter than death itself. Even her hands are {like} chains. Whoever pleases God will escape her, but she will catch whoever continues to sin." 
Ecclesiastes7:27 The spokesman said, ""This is what I've found: I added one thing to another in order to find a reason for things." 
Ecclesiastes7:28 I am still seeking a reason for things, but have not found any. I found one man out of a thousand who had it, but out of all these I didn't find one woman." 
Ecclesiastes7:29 I have found only this: God made people decent, but they looked for many ways {to avoid being decent}.""" 
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is really wise? Who knows how to explain things? Wisdom makes one's face shine, and it changes one's grim look." 
Ecclesiastes8:2 I {advise} you to obey the king's commands because of the oath you took in God's presence. 
Ecclesiastes8:3 Don't be in a hurry to leave the king's service. Don't take part in something evil, because he can do whatever he pleases." 
Ecclesiastes8:4 Since a king's word has such power, no one can ask him what he is doing." 
Ecclesiastes8:5 Whoever obeys his commands will avoid trouble. The mind of a wise person will know the right time and the right way {to act}. 
Ecclesiastes8:6 There is a right time and a right way {to act} in every situation. Yet, a terrible human tragedy hangs over people." 
Ecclesiastes8:7 They don't know what the future will bring. So who can tell them how things will turn out? 
Ecclesiastes8:8 No one has the power to prevent the spirit of life from leaving. No one has control over the day of his own death. There is no way to avoid the war {against death}. Wickedness will not save wicked people {from dying}. 
Ecclesiastes8:9 I have seen all of this, and I have carefully considered all that is done under the sun whenever one person has authority to hurt others." 
Ecclesiastes8:10 Then I saw wicked people given an {honorable} burial. They used to go in and out of the holy place. They were praised in the city for doing such things. Even this is pointless. 
Ecclesiastes8:11 When a sentence against a crime isn't carried out quickly, people are encouraged to commit crimes." 
Ecclesiastes8:12 A sinner may commit a hundred crimes and yet live a long life. Still, I know with certainty that it will go well for those who fear God, because they fear him." 
Ecclesiastes8:13 But it will not go well for the wicked. They will not live any longer. Their lives are like shadows, because they don't fear God." 
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is something being done on earth that is pointless. Righteous people suffer for what the wicked do, and wicked people get what the righteous deserve. I say that even this is pointless." 
Ecclesiastes8:15 So I recommend the enjoyment {of life}. People have nothing better to do under the sun than to eat, drink, and enjoy themselves. This joy will stay with them while they work hard during their brief lives which God has given them under the sun." 
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I carefully considered how to study wisdom and how to look at the work that is done on earth (even going without sleep day and night)," 
Ecclesiastes8:17 then I saw everything that God has done. No one is able to grasp the work that is done under the sun. However hard a person may search for it, he will not find {its meaning}. Even though a wise person claims to know, he is not able to grasp it." 
Ecclesiastes9:1 Now, I have carefully thought about all this, and I explain it in this way: Righteous people and wise people, along with their accomplishments, are in God's hands. No one knows whether there will be love or hatred." 
Ecclesiastes9:2 Everything turns out the same way for everyone. All people will share the same destiny, whether they are righteous, wicked, or good, clean or unclean, whether they offer sacrifices or don't offer sacrifices. Good people are treated like sinners. People who take oaths are treated like those who are afraid to take oaths." 
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is the tragedy of everything that happens under the sun: Everyone shares the same destiny. Moreover, the hearts of mortals are full of evil. Madness is in their hearts while they are still alive. After that, they join the dead." 
Ecclesiastes9:4 But all who are among the living have hope, because a living dog is better than a dead lion." 
Ecclesiastes9:5 The living know that they will die, but the dead don't know anything. There is no more reward for the dead when the memory of them has faded." 
Ecclesiastes9:6 Their love, their hate, and their passions have already vanished. They will never again take part in anything that happens under the sun." 
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go, enjoy eating your food, and drink your wine cheerfully, because God has already accepted what you've done." 
Ecclesiastes9:8 Always wear clean clothes, and never go without lotion on your head." 
Ecclesiastes9:9 Enjoy life with your wife, whom you love, during all your brief, pointless life. God has given you your pointless life under the sun. This is your lot {in life} and what you get for the hard work that you do under the sun." 
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever presents itself for you to do, do it with {all} your might, because there is no work, planning, knowledge, or skill in the grave where you're going." 
Ecclesiastes9:11 I saw something else under the sun. The race isn't {won} by fast runners, or the battle by heroes. Wise people don't necessarily have food. Intelligent people don't necessarily have riches, and skilled people don't necessarily receive special treatment. But time and unpredictable events overtake all of them." 
Ecclesiastes9:12 No one knows when his time will come. Like fish that are caught in a cruel net or birds caught in a snare, humans are trapped by a disaster when it suddenly strikes them." 
Ecclesiastes9:13 I also have seen this example of wisdom under the sun, and it made a deep impression on me." 
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a small town with a few soldiers in it, and a powerful king came to attack it. He surrounded it and blockaded it." 
Ecclesiastes9:15 A poor, wise person was found in that town. He saved the town using his wisdom. But no one remembered that poor person." 
Ecclesiastes9:16 So I said, ""Wisdom is better than strength,"" even though that poor person's wisdom was despised, and no one listened to what he said." 
Ecclesiastes9:17 One should pay more attention to calm words from wise people than shouting from a ruler of fools. 
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war, but one sinner can destroy much that is good." 
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies will make a bottle of perfume stink, and then it is spoiled. A little foolishness outweighs wisdom {and} honor." 
Ecclesiastes10:2 A wise person's heart leads the right way. The heart of a fool leads the wrong way. 
Ecclesiastes10:3 Even when a fool goes walking, he has no sense and shows everyone else that he's a fool." 
Ecclesiastes10:4 If a ruler becomes angry with you, don't resign your position. If you remain calm, you can make up for serious offenses." 
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is a tragedy that I've seen under the sun, an error often made by rulers." 
Ecclesiastes10:6 Foolish people are often given high positions, and rich people are left to fill lower positions." 
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen slaves sitting on horses and influential people going on foot like slaves. 
Ecclesiastes10:8 Whoever digs a pit may fall into it. Whoever breaks through a stone wall may be bitten by a snake. 
Ecclesiastes10:9 Whoever works in a stone quarry may get hurt. Whoever splits wood may be injured. 
Ecclesiastes10:10 If an ax is blunt and the edge isn't sharpened, then one has to use more strength. But wisdom prepares the way for success." 
Ecclesiastes10:11 If a snake bites before it has been charmed, then there is no advantage in being a snake charmer." 
Ecclesiastes10:12 A wise person's words win favors, but a fool's lips are self-destructive." 
Ecclesiastes10:13 A fool starts out by talking foolishness and ends up saying crazy things that are dangerous. 
Ecclesiastes10:14 He never stops talking. No one knows what the future will bring, or what will happen after {death}. Who can say!" 
Ecclesiastes10:15 Fools wear themselves out with hard work, because they don't even know the way to town." 
Ecclesiastes10:16 How horrible it will be for any country where the king used to be a servant and where the high officials throw parties in the morning. 
Ecclesiastes10:17 A country is blessed when the king is from a noble family and when the high officials eat at the right time in order to get strength and not to get drunk. 
Ecclesiastes10:18 A roof sags because of laziness. A house leaks because of idle hands. 
Ecclesiastes10:19 A meal is made for laughter, and wine makes life pleasant, but money is the answer for everything." 
Ecclesiastes10:20 Don't curse the king even in your thoughts, and don't curse rich people even in your bedroom. A bird may carry your words, or some winged creature may repeat what you say." 
Ecclesiastes11:1 Throw your bread on the surface of the water, because you will find it again after many days." 
Ecclesiastes11:2 Divide what you have into seven parts, or even into eight, because you don't know what disaster may happen on earth." 
Ecclesiastes11:3 If the clouds are full of rain, they will let it pour down on the earth. If a tree falls north or south, the tree will remain where it fell." 
Ecclesiastes11:4 Whoever watches the wind will never plant. Whoever looks at the clouds will never harvest. 
Ecclesiastes11:5 Just as you don't know how the breath of life enters the limbs of a child within its mother's womb, you also don't understand how God, who made everything, works." 
Ecclesiastes11:6 Plant your seed in the morning, and don't let your hands rest until evening. You don't know whether this field or that field will be profitable or whether both of them will {turn out} equally well." 
Ecclesiastes11:7 Light is sweet, and it is good for one's eyes to see the sun." 
Ecclesiastes11:8 Even though people may live for many years, they should enjoy every one of them. But they should also remember there will be many dark days. Everything that is coming is pointless." 
Ecclesiastes11:9 You young people should enjoy yourselves while you're young. You should let your hearts make you happy when you're young. Follow wherever your heart leads you and whatever your eyes see. But realize that God will make you give an account for all these things when he judges everyone. 
Ecclesiastes11:10 Get rid of what troubles you or wears down your body, because childhood and youth are pointless." 
Ecclesiastes12:1 Remember your Creator when you are young, before the days of trouble come and the years catch up with you. They will make you say, ""I have found no pleasure in them.""" 
Ecclesiastes12:2 Remember your Creator before the sun, the light, the moon, and the stars turn dark, {and} the clouds come back with rain." 
Ecclesiastes12:3 Remember your Creator when those who guard the house tremble, strong men are stooped over, the women at the mill stop grinding because there are so few of them, {and} those who look out of the windows see a dim light." 
Ecclesiastes12:4 Remember your Creator when the doors to the street are closed, the sound of the mill is muffled, you are startled at the sound of a bird, {and} those who sing songs become quiet." 
Ecclesiastes12:5 Remember your Creator when someone is afraid of heights and of dangers along the road, the almond tree blossoms, the grasshopper drags itself along, {and} the caper bush has {no} fruit. Mortals go to their eternal rest, and mourners go out in the streets." 
Ecclesiastes12:6 Remember your Creator before the silver cord is snapped, the golden bowl is broken, the pitcher is smashed near the spring, and the water wheel is broken at the cistern." 
Ecclesiastes12:7 Then the dust {of mortals} goes back to the ground as it was before, and the breath of life goes back to God who gave it." 
Ecclesiastes12:8 Absolutely pointless! says the spokesman. Everything is pointless! 
Ecclesiastes12:9 Besides being wise, the spokesman also taught the people what he knew. He very carefully thought about it, studied it, and arranged it in many proverbs." 
Ecclesiastes12:10 The spokesman tried to find just the right words. He wrote the words of truth very carefully. 
Ecclesiastes12:11 Words from wise people are like spurs. Their collected sayings are like nails that have been driven in firmly. They come from one shepherd. 
Ecclesiastes12:12 Be warned, my children, against anything more than these. People never stop writing books. Too much studying will wear out your body." 
Ecclesiastes12:13 After having heard it all, this is the conclusion: Fear God, and keep his commands, because this applies to everyone." 
Ecclesiastes12:14 God will certainly judge everything that is done. This includes every secret thing, whether it is good or bad." 
SongOfSongs1:1 The most beautiful song of Solomon. 
SongOfSongs1:2 Bride Let him kiss me with the kisses of his mouth. Your expressions of love are better than wine," 
SongOfSongs1:3 better than the fragrance of cologne. (Cologne should be named after you.) No wonder the young women love you! 
SongOfSongs1:4 Take me with you. Let's run away. The king has brought me into his private rooms. The chorus of young women We will celebrate and rejoice with you. We will praise your expressions of love more than wine. How right it is that the young women love you! 
SongOfSongs1:5 Bride Young women of Jerusalem, I am dark and lovely like Kedar's tents, like Solomon's curtains." 
SongOfSongs1:6 Stop staring at me because I am so dark. The sun has tanned me. My brothers were angry with me. They made me the caretaker of the vineyards. I have not even taken care of my own vineyard. 
SongOfSongs1:7 Please tell me, you whom I love, where do you graze your flock? Where does your flock lie down at noon? {Tell me,} or I will be considered a prostitute {wandering} among the flocks of your companions." 
SongOfSongs1:8 The chorus of young women If you do not know, most beautiful of women, follow the tracks of the flocks, and graze your young goats near the shepherds' tents." 
SongOfSongs1:9 Groom My true love, I compare you to a mare among Pharaoh's stallions." 
SongOfSongs1:10 Your cheeks are lovely with ornaments, your neck with strings of pearls." 
SongOfSongs1:11 The chorus of young women We will make gold ornaments with silver beads for you. 
SongOfSongs1:12 Bride While the king is at his table, my perfume fills the air with its fragrance." 
SongOfSongs1:13 My beloved is a pouch of myrrh that lies at night between my breasts. 
SongOfSongs1:14 My beloved is a bouquet of henna flowers in the vineyards of En Gedi. 
SongOfSongs1:15 Groom Look at you! You are beautiful, my true love! Look at you! You are so beautiful! Your eyes are like doves!" 
SongOfSongs1:16 Bride Look at you! You are handsome, my beloved, so pleasing to me! The leaf-scattered ground will be our couch." 
SongOfSongs1:17 The cedars will be the walls of our house. The cypress trees will be our rafters. 
SongOfSongs2:1 I am a rose of Sharon, a lily {growing} in the valleys." 
SongOfSongs2:2 Groom Like a lily among thorns, so is my true love among the young women." 
SongOfSongs2:3 Bride Like an apple tree among the trees in the forest, so is my beloved among the young men. I want to sit in his shadow. His fruit tastes sweet to me." 
SongOfSongs2:4 He leads me into a banquet room and looks at me with love. 
SongOfSongs2:5 Strengthen me with raisins and refresh me with apples because I am weak from love. 
SongOfSongs2:6 His left hand is under my head. His right hand caresses me. 
SongOfSongs2:7 Young women of Jerusalem, swear to me by the gazelles or by the does in the field that you will not awaken love or arouse love before its proper time." 
SongOfSongs2:8 Bride I hear my beloved's voice. Look! Here he comes, sprinting over the mountains, racing over the hills." 
SongOfSongs2:9 My beloved is like a gazelle or a young stag. Look! There he stands behind our wall, peeking through the window, looking through the lattice." 
SongOfSongs2:10 My beloved said to me, ""Get up, my true love, my beautiful one, and come with me." 
SongOfSongs2:11 Look! The winter is past. The rain is over and gone. 
SongOfSongs2:12 Blossoms appear in the land. The time of the songbird has arrived. The cooing of the mourning dove is heard in our land. 
SongOfSongs2:13 The green figs ripen. The grapevines bloom and give off a fragrance. Get up, my true love, my beautiful one, and come with me." 
SongOfSongs2:14 My dove, in the hiding places of the rocky crevices, in the secret places of the cliffs, let me see your figure and hear your voice. Your voice is sweet, and your figure is lovely.""" 
SongOfSongs2:15 Catch the foxes for us, the little foxes that ruin vineyards. Our vineyards are blooming." 
SongOfSongs2:16 My beloved is mine, and I am his. He is the one who grazes his flock among the lilies." 
SongOfSongs2:17 When the day brings a cooling breeze and the shadows flee, turn around, my beloved. Run like a gazelle or a young stag on the mountains that separate us!" 
SongOfSongs3:1 Bride Night after night on my bed I looked for the one I love. I looked for him but did not find him. 
SongOfSongs3:2 I will get up now and roam around the city, in the streets, and in the squares. I will look for the one I love. I looked for him but did not find him." 
SongOfSongs3:3 The watchmen making their rounds in the city found me. {I asked,} ""Have you seen the one I love?""" 
SongOfSongs3:4 I had just left them when I found the one I love. I held on to him and would not let him go until I had brought him into my mother's house, into the bedroom of the one who conceived me." 
SongOfSongs3:5 Young women of Jerusalem, swear to me by the gazelles or by the does in the field, that you will not awaken love or arouse love before its proper time." 
SongOfSongs3:6 The chorus of young women Who is this young woman coming up from the wilderness like clouds of smoke? She is perfumed with myrrh and incense made from the merchants' scented powders. 
SongOfSongs3:7 Look! Solomon's sedan chair! Sixty soldiers from the army of Israel surround it. 
SongOfSongs3:8 All of them are skilled in using swords, experienced in combat. Each one has his sword at his side and guards against the terrors of the night." 
SongOfSongs3:9 King Solomon had a carriage made for himself from the wood of Lebanon. 
SongOfSongs3:10 He had its posts made out of silver, its top out of gold, its seat out of purple fabric. Its inside—with inlaid scenes of love— was made by the young women of Jerusalem." 
SongOfSongs3:11 Young women of Zion, come out and look at King Solomon! Look at his crown, the crown his mother placed on him on his wedding day, his day of joyful delight." 
SongOfSongs4:1 Groom Look at you! You are beautiful, my true love. Look at you! You are so beautiful. Your eyes behind your veil are like doves. Your hair is like a flock of goats moving down Mount Gilead." 
SongOfSongs4:2 Your teeth are like a flock of sheep about to be sheared, sheep that come up from the washing. All of them bear twins, and not one has lost its young." 
SongOfSongs4:3 Your lips are like scarlet thread. Your mouth is lovely. Your temples behind your veil are like slices of pomegranate. 
SongOfSongs4:4 Your neck is like David's beautifully-designed tower. A thousand round shields belonging to soldiers are hung on it. 
SongOfSongs4:5 Your breasts are like two fawns, like twin gazelles grazing among the lilies." 
SongOfSongs4:6 When the day brings a cool breeze and the shadows flee, I will go to the mountain of myrrh and the hill of incense." 
SongOfSongs4:7 You are beautiful in every way, my true love. There is no blemish on you." 
SongOfSongs4:8 You will come with me from Lebanon, from Lebanon as my bride. You will travel with me from the peak of Mount Amana, from the mountain peaks in Senir and Hermon, from the lairs of lions, from the mountains of leopards." 
SongOfSongs4:9 My bride, my sister, you have charmed me. You have charmed me with a single glance from your eyes, with a single strand of your necklace." 
SongOfSongs4:10 How beautiful are your expressions of love, my bride, my sister! How much better are your expressions of love than wine and the fragrance of your perfume than any spice." 
SongOfSongs4:11 Your lips drip honey, my bride. Honey and milk are under your tongue. The fragrance of your clothing is like the fragrance of Lebanon." 
SongOfSongs4:12 My bride, my sister is a garden that is locked, a garden that is locked, a spring that is sealed." 
SongOfSongs4:13 You are paradise that produces pomegranates and the best fruits, henna flowers and nard," 
SongOfSongs4:14 nard and saffron, calamus, cinnamon, and all kinds of incense, myrrh, aloes, and all the best spices." 
SongOfSongs4:15 {You are} a spring for gardens, a well of living water flowing from Lebanon." 
SongOfSongs4:16 Bride Awake, north wind! Come, south wind! Blow on my garden! Let its spices flow from it. Let my beloved come to his garden, and let him eat his own precious fruit." 
SongOfSongs5:1 Groom My bride, my sister, I will come to my garden. I will gather my myrrh with my spice. I will eat my honeycomb with my honey. I will drink my wine with my milk. Eat, my friends! Drink and become intoxicated with expressions of love!" 
SongOfSongs5:2 Bride I sleep, but my mind is awake. Listen! My beloved is knocking. Groom Open to me, my true love, my sister, my dove, my perfect one. My head is wet with dew, my hair with the dewdrops of night." 
SongOfSongs5:3 Bride I have taken off my clothes! Why should I put them on {again}? I have washed my feet! Why should I get them dirty {again}? 
SongOfSongs5:4 My beloved put his hand through the keyhole. My heart throbbed for him. 
SongOfSongs5:5 I got up to open for my beloved. My hands dripped with myrrh, and my fingers were drenched with liquid myrrh, on the handles of the lock." 
SongOfSongs5:6 I opened for my beloved, but my beloved had turned away. He was gone! I almost died when he left. I looked for him, but I did not find him. I called for him, but he did not answer me." 
SongOfSongs5:7 The watchmen making their rounds in the city found me. They struck me! They wounded me! Those watchmen on the walls took my robe from me! 
SongOfSongs5:8 Young women of Jerusalem, swear to me that if you find my beloved you will tell him I am hopelessly lovesick." 
SongOfSongs5:9 The chorus of young women Most beautiful of women, what makes your beloved better than any other beloved? What makes your beloved better than any other beloved that you make us swear this way?" 
SongOfSongs5:10 Bride My beloved is dazzling yet ruddy. He stands out among 10,000 men." 
SongOfSongs5:11 His head is the finest gold. His hair is wavy, black as a raven." 
SongOfSongs5:12 His eyes are set like doves bathing in milk. 
SongOfSongs5:13 His cheeks are like a garden of spices, a garden that produces scented herbs. His lips are lilies that drip with myrrh." 
SongOfSongs5:14 His hands are disks of gold set with emerald. His chest is a block of ivory covered with sapphires. 
SongOfSongs5:15 His legs are columns of marble set on bases of pure gold. His form is like Lebanon, choice as the cedars." 
SongOfSongs5:16 His mouth is sweet in every way. Everything about him is desirable! This is my beloved, and this is my friend, young women of Jerusalem."

SongOfSongs6:2 Bride My beloved went to his garden, to the beds of spices, to graze his flock in the gardens and gather lilies." 
SongOfSongs6:3 I am my beloved's, and my beloved is mine. He is the one who grazes his flock among the lilies." 
SongOfSongs6:4 Groom You are beautiful, my true love, like Tirzah, lovely like Jerusalem, awe-inspiring like those great cities." 
SongOfSongs6:5 Turn your eyes away from me. They enchant me! Your hair is like a flock of goats moving down from Gilead. 
SongOfSongs6:6 Your teeth are like a flock of sheep, sheep that come up from the washing. All of them bear twins, and not one has lost its young." 
SongOfSongs6:7 Your temples behind your veil are like slices of pomegranate. 
SongOfSongs6:8 There are 60 queens, 80 concubines, and countless virgins,"

SongOfSongs7:2 Your navel is a round bowl. May it always be filled with spiced wine. Your waist is a bundle of wheat enclosed in lilies. 
SongOfSongs7:3 Your breasts are like two fawns, twins of a gazelle." 
SongOfSongs7:4 Your neck is like an ivory tower. Your eyes are like pools in Heshbon, pools by the gate of Bath Rabbim. Your nose is like a Lebanese tower facing Damascus." 
SongOfSongs7:5 You hold your head as high as Mount Carmel. Your dangling curls are royal beauty. Your flowing locks could hold a king captive.

SongOfSongs7:7 Young woman, your figure is like a palm tree, and your breasts are like its clusters."

SongOfSongs8:1 If only you were my brother, one who nursed at my mother's breasts. If I saw you on the street, I would kiss you, and no one would look down on me." 
SongOfSongs8:2 I would lead you. I would bring you into my mother's house. (She is the one who was my teacher.) I would give you some spiced wine to drink, some juice squeezed from my pomegranates."

SongOfSongs8:7 Raging water cannot extinguish love, and rivers will never wash it away. If a man exchanged all his family's wealth for love, people would utterly despise him."

SongOfSongs8:12 My own vineyard is in front of me. That 25 pounds is yours, Solomon, and 5 pounds go to those who take care of its fruit."
